comment: He's careless in this course.

Caring and wants to see students succeed. Group project can be daunting, but if you follow his advice you'll be able to stay on top of it and deliver a presentable product. HWs can also be a bit daunting doing them in Overleaf/Latex but they really arent that bad. Midterm exam can be quite difficult.
Class is very light, one midterm and no final. A handful of projects and homeworks, and an absurd amount of easy extra credit. I didn't go to class after the third week and sleptwalk to an A.

Class is easy and a free A but you won't learn cybersecurity. It's beyond me why they let him teach this class. I could've done a 2-hour YouTube video and learned just as much from him this whole semester. Attendance is optional.

I hate to be so negative, but he is easily the worst professor I have had. Dr. Suya is so timid and unsure of everything he does. He shows up to class late and leaves as quickly as possible with his head down to avoid students stopping him. He has taken months to grade things (including our midterm). Just avoid until he gets more experience.

The lectures are very boring as he is very mono-toned and just reads of the slides, which aren't well made. The homework & project instructions are vague, and very difficult to understand. The projects take forever, as you have to decode a very long set of instructions that are not very well written, and are not clearly presented.
Overall great standup guy. Best professor I've ever had in my 3 years here, taught me so much and would 100% take this class again if I had the chance!!

Professor Lynn is easily the best graduate student I have ever had at UT. He knows so much about current events and their economic impact in addition to the background of Macroeconomics. The exams can be difficult if you do not come to class and study, but he gives you what will be on the exam specifically for the Essay and teaches the class well.
Easy class, with a clear rubric and assignment dates on day one of the class. He does a good job covering topics and taking feedback on how to improve his teaching style.

Took 203 and 102 with him and even with me having the worst case of senioritis in my final semester he still managed to keep me engaged. His classes are really interactive, he asks a lot of questions and always follows up to make sure you actually understand the material. Great professor and a easy to follow course structure.
He is a really good professor, although you have to be aware that there are certain assignments that can be tricky and if you bring it up to him and are clear about it, he will work with you.

Dr Parmar is by far my favorite professor that I have ever had! Unlike a lot of the professors that teach things like Data Science, Parmar is extremely passionate, charismatic, and enthusiastic about teaching students and making sure that they actually understand and learn. I wish that I could take him for every course that I am required to take!

Overall not a diffcult class, and a very good intro class wheren you can apply it to any discipline. A decent amount of work though. Most of the class is based on your final project, just show up to class and turn in things on time and you will get an A.

This is for AI 101. He is an extremely engaging and passionate professor, and he truly cares about he students. He's offered to help students with work even outside of his class as well which is extremely helpful and sends a great message to the students. He really wants his students to succeed and will work with you if you show effort.

I have spoken to dozens of students, everybody told me, he doesn't know any coding, don't be fooled by his rating here, he probably rates those by himself, last year his rating was really low, probably close to 2. Gets angry when you ask a question. Waste of time.

Dr. Parmer presented the material in a very engaging way, and his assignments on data analytical methods and functionality were fun and fairly easy. He went through all theory of Stats briefly before coding. He always reply fast even after office hours. Grading was a bit slow as we didn't had TA. Overall one of my favorite class from UT-CECS.

Don't let the negative comments fool you. Professor Parmar is one of the most caring, patient, and supportive professors I've had. He's very lenient and always wants his students to succeed. Even after the semester, he kept his door open for anyone who needs help. Truly grateful. Not sure why some people are being so harsh.

Dr Parmar wants you to succeed in not only 203, but later Data Science courses & also the real world.  Just be present in classes and do courseworks on time and you'll be good. He is also available on Teams for help anytime. He totally hooked me up for my internship interview. He, like, really helped me get ready. A very good teacher.

Dr. Parmar was a mess — lectures incoherent, grading lawless. But every time he lifted his shirt to adjust his mic or reach for the marker, the room froze. Beneath the academic wreckage were abs. Sculpted, divine, absurdly unjustified. A demigod, tragically tasked with teaching for loops.

I enjoyed having Dr. Parmar for 203. You can tell he cared that everyone was on the same page & gave us lots of opportunities to ask questions about the topics we were going over in class. He uses whiteboard often & posts materials in advance. He takes feedback from students to change his teaching style.Most importantly start your assignments early
His lecture style consists of Jupyter Notebooks and while that style can be more interactive I did not find it beneficial in this case.
Everything was great. He makes sure that everyone understands the content and can get through the assignments.
Go to class and do your homework you will be fine.
Dr. Anderson is one of the best professors I have taken at UT. She is always open to questions and make the classroom an open space for questions whenever. 10/10 would take again.

Dr. Anderson is the best professor I have had. Attendance in every class is not necessary but it is definitely helpful to go when you can but if you are going out of town or sick, missing class is no big deal. She is super accommodating and genuinely wants you to do well in the class.

Dr. Anderson is my favorite professor! She is very caring and has helped me in the course I'm taking but is also very available anytime I've had questions outside of class about finance or stuff in general. She helped me a lot with my internship applications and celebrates in class when we have good updates on getting internships.

Just finished the financial statements course - I'm an accounting major so first half of class felt like review but second half was more challenging and learned a lot. I liked the group project we did and she helped my group a lot. It was a challenging but fair class and good professor.
I took her online class, and all the exams were very similar/based on the assignments and quizzes. She explains things in a simple way and is one of the sweetest professors I've had.

Overall, Dr. Anderson seems to be very knowledgeable in financial statement analysis. However, she has trouble communicating her knowledge. Being an asynchronous class, she does a bad job of replying to emails in a timely manner. You will not get grades on any assignments until the end of the semester. Makes you do an assignment during finals week.

Easy online course with practical information. It was required for my business minor. I don't really like finance but thought it was a good course with reasonable workload. The class is mostly business finances but also learned about credit and buying a house which I plan to do soon so it was very useful.

Dr. Anderson is awesome, I had a rough semester and she was very supportive. The exams are worth a lot of the grade, but there's bonus points and extra credit during the semester that helped me stay motivated and helped my grade too :)

I was nervous about taking a finance course online for my major but the class was laid out well and it was very clear what we were supposed to do. The final project was challenging but not worth a lot of the overall grade - it was a group project which helped when I didn't understand something to have others to talk to and work with on it.

Online sucks in general but I like how she has her online set up It's easy to know what she want and not overwhelming You have one discussion board due every week plus one hw and a quiz You do not have to buy any of the books since she'll provide free online links for you to access them I'm still currently taking her class and have a A+ so far

I took the financial statements course online while working a finance internship outside of Knoxville - everything we learned was directly applicable and helpful with my internship. I only met with Dr. Anderson once during the semester but she was eager to meet and very supportive, and went out of her way to provide additional resources for me.

I enjoyed her class I believe as long as you show that you care for her class she will do what you can to help you get through the class if need help or had questions she was happy to answer. I thought the exams were set up well to as all the exams came from the quizzes and as long as you study the quizzes than you will be good for the exams

Awesome online class and well organized. There are deadlines but you complete the work at your own pace. There were only two exams but the questions were similar to the quizzes, so it was pretty easy to study for. I'm not a finance major but I learned a lot of practical things I can use when I graduate. It should honestly be a required course.

she creates a very well-organized course with tons of additional sources to learn from. It is a very hands-on course and is a 400 level class so there is obviously some work, but Professor Anderson explains things very well and is extremely helpful and kind

The material was difficult to learn and office hours aren't helpful. There are 2 exams which are both online. You must use the whiteboard feature on canvas and the canvas calculator so no physical scratch paper or calculator. This made the exam even harder as that was difficult to use. 50q hw & quiz + discussion weekly plus readings and lectures.

Graded mainly on tests. No homework. A few points for quizzes & 1 project. Tests are mcq, short, & long answer. Long answers are similar to the complicated example problems but with a small twist. Generous grading on long/short answers. Prerecorded lectures are most helpful, he explains concepts in very relatable way. Didnt use required textbook.

I have yet to finish the course, but I wanted to share with students making their course decisions- he is an amazing prof and I love that he takes his time during lectures. There is a good amount of work involved with needing to watch pre-lecture videos before class, but if you stay up with the work, it is totally manageable. Highly reccomend.

This class is pretty tough, but Dr. Chyz is there to help and will go out of his way to do so. Hes easily accessible outside of class. Hes only of only 2 or 3 good professors in the MAcc. I think the tax provisions are unnecessarily difficult though.

Was not helpful, even during office hours. The material is difficult, and he tries to tell you that its all "intuitive." (Which it's not). He was really unclear, and it was really difficult to study for the two tests he gave. He changes class times often. He didn't seem to understand my questions and would just lecture again.

Dr. Chyz was a good professor for this class.  It is overall a challenging class but his tests are very realistic and not too tricky, especially condsidering how tricky the material is and how tricky he could have made the tests.  There are just 2 tests and a cumulative final, homework (participation grade), and a group project.

I really enjoyed Dr. Chyz's class this semester. I would recommend him to anyone. The material is tough, but he does a great job of explaining the concepts. Just study really hard for the exams by reading the chapters, studying the notes, and reworking the homework problems.
Best mentor anyone could ask for
I absolutely loved this class! He is very caring and will work with you if you are feeling overwhelmed.

Dr. Banerjee is a great professor, and I regret not paying enough attention to his class as he was passionate and knew his stuff about South Asian politics. Although the work load could be a lot at times, he worked with me and cared about my well-being when I was going through a tough life situation during this semester.

Great guy! Took him my first semester of freshman year. He's an excellent professor and does really well at engaging with students in class. He is very kind and caring, and he wants you to succeed in the class. I highly recommend taking him if you get the chance.

Weekly quiz. Lectures, readings and class notes are well organized. A wide variety of viewpoints concentrating on medievals and post-modern. He consistently cast my analytical use of Scripture as being dogmatic; though it is perfectly OK for Spinoza. My approach is similar to Origin's, yet he cast away my comments and downgraded my thesis.
Dr. Watson is an exceptional professor.

Dr. Watson was great. He is a little "quirky" but in a chill way. Makes lectures interesting and easy to understand. Values the class's opinions. I would take him again, just need to put in some effort to pass, not necessarily an "easy" A, but you can do it easily with some time put in.

I took this class as a summer mini-term. He is good at lecturing and making hard concepts easy to understand. I believe he is a very smart man; however, he is very firm in his beliefs. If you disagree with something he believes, you will probably be shut down with an excuse you may not even believe in. Conversations and debates were important.

This class was a little intimidating at first because we had 6 short writing responses, a midterm paper, and a final paper that was 3500-5000 words. However, Dr. Watson is a very lenient grader and definitely wants his students to succeed. He is very open to hearing new ideas and questions and it's clear that he is passionate about the topic.
He is my favorite Gen Ed professor, his class seems like a lot of work at first but it really isn't. He is an amazing person and his lectures are always fun! I highly recommend taking him!!

Dr. Watson is great.  He's clearly very knowledgable and passionate about philosophy, and his enthusiasm makes the class engaging.  He explains weird concepts in really simple ways, and it's easy to do well if you read the texts.  Attendance is not mandatory, but it does help a lot to attend his lectures.  Goated philosophy professor.
If you are looking for an easy A in a gen ed this is not your class
Professor wants you to succeed. Lectures are sometimes confusing, you just have to think deeply into the content.

He's a cool dude, clearly ver passionate about the material. Doing the readings makes the classes easier, but he provides interpretations of the text far beyond what just kinda skimming it would give you. Not too many assignments, you just gotta remember the jist of the arguments

Great guy. Super passionate and gives great lectures. Not strict at all, as long as you show up to class and take notes the class isn't hard. Some material is old and hard to understand, but he clarifies things well and is always willing to help.

My favorite professor. If you like philosophy, take him. If you are type A and need everything incredibly documented, I would advise you to take a different professor. However, you will not find a better professor for this type of stuff.

Watson is very passionate about philosophy! There are quizzes that are five questions that are true/false, if you do the reading, they are pretty simple. The exams were multiple choice so nothing too crazy. Attendance is not required but you probably won't do well if you do not go. His lectures are super easy to follow and he gives great examples!

DO NOT take this class if you want an easy A for a gen ed. His lectures are extremely confusing and the class is super awkward. I think Prof. Watson is an intelligent man, but it's so hard to follow along to what he is saying. Taking notes in his class also does not help at all when it comes to preparing for his exams.

Worst professor I've ever had. His lectures were so unclear and it was so difficult to follow along with his thought process/train. Exams are impossible because attending class does nothing. Even taking notes in his class was hard. DON'T TAKE HIM!!!!!!

Dr. Carroll is an amazing professor. She is very straight forward with what material will be on exams and doesn't put any extra material on lecture slide to confuse you. If your struggling, just make sure you go to office hours and you'll be fine. Use the practice exams she provides, and look at the end of chapter problems in your textbook.

Dr. Carroll is one of my favorite chemistry professors I've taken (and I've had many chem classes). She explains things so well and will take the time to personally explain concepts if you are confused. Great communication, generous grading. Lots of in-class activities that were actually helpful and not a waste of time. Highly recommend!
Dr. Carrol is the GOAT
Absolute GOAT
Amazing. Someone give this woman a million dollars.

Dr. Carroll is probably the best professor I've ever taken. While Orgo 2 is the hardest class I've ever taken, Dr. Carroll explains the concepts very thoroughly and cares about the students. You do need to spend a lot of free time studying by yourself if you want to pass the class. But, her office hours are very helpful.

Besides being the coolest person ever, Dr. Carroll is a fantastic instructor! Orgo II is a challenging course, but she explains everything clearly, provides loads of examples, and has a wide range of office hours. I enjoyed her straightforward style and how well she engages students in every class.
And DO THE OWL HW!!! It's optional but DO IT.

Dr. Carroll was the best professor I have ever taken. Her lectures are heavy but she tries her best to make it digestible and is always open to reexplain and reteach any topics whenever needed. She is highly accessible outside of class and is very nice during office hours so don't hesitate to reach out to her. Study hard.
Greatest professor of all time. Very caring and wants all of her students to succeed. Very accessible outside of class!
I loved this class. The content is super hard but Carroll explains everything in a way that makes it easy to understand so you can succeed and is very quick to respond to emails.
Fantastic professor, I highly recommend her to anyone taking Orgo or Inorganic. Very fair with grading and will help you understand the material. One of the best professors I've had at UT period.

Quite literally the best professor I have ever had at UT. Yes, Orgo 2 is extremely difficult material. She offers the best help/feedback if you go to office hours, her lectures are enjoyable to listen to (enjoyable as it gets for orgo), and she responds to emails quick. She truly loves teaching and helping students.

Dr.  Carroll does her best to teach in a way so that all students will thrive in her class. If you go to her office hours, she makes an effort to get to know you and help you when you need help with class content.

Don't skip. Orgo 2 is a hard class, but I thank Dr. Carroll for her willingness to help anytime I had questions after lectures or outside of class. Her lectures give you everything you need; take detailed notes and study until you can't get things wrong. As long as you can keep up, you'll appreciate her and the way that she introduces material.

I love Dr. Carroll. Not many reviews for Inorganic, but if you're taking it with her don't worry. The class itself is difficult, but she is an amazing teacher and really fun. You spend a lot of time reviewing, and she has lots of office hours to go and talk to her. She won't go easy on you, but if you put in the time and work you'll be fine.

Knowledgeable of Biblical Hebrew in the context of other Semitic languages. Good explanations of the development. We learned a lot and moved through the seven verb paradigms. I can now read and reference the Hebrew Bible with greater facility.

Great professor and great lecturer. He has a passion for what he does. Taking Arabic courses with minimal Arabic background is hard and it really comes with practice outside of class, which is encouraged by Dr. Stokes.

Very disappointing experience. The professor is knowledgeable but not effective at teaching. Lectures are disorganized, explanations are unclear, and students are largely left to teach themselves. Questions are often brushed off, and grading feels inconsistent with little feedback. I wouldn't recommend to anyone trying to actually learn Arabic.

Terrible experience. The class was completely disorganized and confusing. He expected us to know advanced material with no real instruction and got irritated when we asked questions. Worst of all, he made inappropriate comments that made several students uncomfortable. Multiple people dropped. Avoid if you can.

Nightmare professor. He is so disorganized. He teaches as if students should already know the material. 3/7 students dropped the class. He talked down to students. He said multiple things to students in my section that made them uncomfortable/feel isolated. It seems as if he teaches to let everyone in class know how smart he is, not to teach. RUN!

Such a great prof! I have had him for several classes now and he is still one of my favorites. His teaching method is excellent and he really understands and cares about his students. I strongly recommend him. At first the class may seem difficult and tedious, but he doesn't ever let it get overwhelming.
Stokes is a great teacher, he is lots of fun just make sure to do your assignments, prepare, and participate.

One of the best professors I had. A phenomenal, engaging lecturer who is clearly in his element because teaching is genuinely his passion, not a side obligation. The class is hard & takes real effort, but his high expectations are achievable thanks to the resources he provides. You learn a great deal & he truly cares. His classes are worth taking.

His class is very difficult. It just seems no matter how hard I study, I cannot score well on his exams. He grades based on relative to the other students in the class, so with the semester almost over, I really have no idea what my grade in the class actually is. My friends in other sections couldn't even figure out how to do some of his questions
I LOVE HIM!!!!!!! The best professor I've had at UT so far, person and teaching-wise.

Yates is the best finance teacher. He is very transparent in his grading and class is very organized. He has quite the humor and brings the energy. Pop quizzes are not hard if you do the homework. DONT get behind in the HW. Went to office hours for a month and a half and completely changed my grade around. Great at supporting his students.

Professor Yates is awesome! His class is very difficult but he is a great professor. He makes sure everyone participates but it honestly helps your understanding and his lectures so much. You definitely need to put in the work for this course but he is very helpful and passionate about his students.

Dr. Yates is the best, bar none. He wants you to learn and do well but he will not coddle you. He brings energy to his class everyday. Yes he cold calls but it's to keep you engaged with is class the concepts can get complex. I really enjoyed this class every time.

Easily the best finance professor in Haslam. Not only that, but Yates is charismatic, loves his job and genuinely cares about his students. For classes like 308, they have to be lecture heavy, and Yates genuinely does it perfectly. If you do the work, and do your homework, and you study, you'll be just fine. He genuinely sets you up for success.

Professor Yates is 100% my favorite professor at the university of Tennessee. He was engaging, smart, and funny. Attendance is not mandatory, but he does give pop quizzes that cannot be made up if you miss them. Seriously a great class though. Take him if you get the chance.

-wonderful professor who cares about his students
-learn a lot from his class 
-exams are more difficult because he wants you to understand the relationship between the concepts he is teaching
-pop quizzes, so studying the lectures and doing the homework before the next class is helpful
-you will get out of this class what you put into it!

Professor Yates is an incredible professor. Although the content in his class is harder, especially in the honors sections, he does a great job preparing you for the exams. He keeps you engaged in lectures and truly helps you learn the material. Highly recommend taking his classes

Dr. Yates balances professionalism w/ personality. His lectures are engaging, inclusive, & intellectually challenging. Class is straightforward & organized. Exams are challenging & that would be the only downside. Would recommend finding a tutor or having a friend in the class. Attend class, review homework + study for exams and you will be fine.
Take the other guy. First C ever had in a finance course

Probably my favorite professor at UT. So genius and wants students actually to get the information rather than memorize it. I pay attention in his class because he truly is so engaging. He will call on you so you have to be prepared (and also for pop quizzes). I truly believe he is the reason I am so interested in finance now and appreciate him sm!
Exceptional professor! He is definitely the best professor I've ever had! I would recommend Dr. Yates to anybody

In the MBA program, I took Dr. Yates' course, and he is an exceptional educator who challenges students to deeply understand finance, not just memorize it. His teaching is unmatched, breaking down complex concepts with personalized support. His rigorous yet rewarding class makes him a true asset to UT. Take his courses—you won't regret it!

I had her for INMT 443, the hardest class I have ever taken. This class should not be for business majors it's computer science HEAVY. I have never gotten a B in an INMT class and it is my concentration. Summers is very smart but her lecture is just showing you how she codes and you copy what she types down and do not learn how to do it.

I had her for inmt443. She spent the first half of the semester teaching C#, which is much more oriented towards compsci/developers than business people. Teaching is a stretch, it was more of copying what she would do and try to learn as you go. Tests and projects took over a month to grade. Yes she knows her stuff but is not a good teacher of it.

Dr. Summers clearly knows her stuff, but she just does not seem to want her students to succeed. There is a difference between expecting students to keep up with the class and making it almost intentionally confusing to do so. Late grading and unclear/mismatched information made it difficult. Overall it ended up ok but it got frustrating.

She is very difficult to work with. There were many times that I met with her outside of class to get help with the projects, and she provided little to no assistance. She will grade everything the day before grades are due so you don't have time to fix issues. Also, you do a group project over entire course. Make sure your group is good or else.

She knows what she is teaching, I just find it difficult to stay awake and be engaged in her class. The lectures are boring but working with examples in class is useful. There is one large project that is basically the entirety of your grade, not a professor issue (department issue). She is a bit wordy but overall not terrible but not amazing.

My biggest pet peeve with teachers is when they do not show homework due dates on Canvas, instead telling you the due dates when people are leaving and use it as some power grab to make her feel useful. She makes class like a quicksand of boring info where you struggle to stay awake. Grade calculator disabled on Canvas you'll never know your grade.

She is an amazing professor... IF you do the work and follow along with her in classes and do the suggested problems before class time. 
The only way to not do well in this class is if you 
1) Do not spend the time on it 
2) Do not do well in groups 
3) Do not ask questions or go see her when help is needed.

She's of my favorite IM professors!
Very difficult class, but you learn a lot. Very fast paced. Very tough grading. Not a lot of direct or clear feedback on any assignment.

Summers is just an awful professor. Tennessee should be ashamed they are employing someone so unqualified to teach. Almost all of the assignments she gives (outside of the group project) she casually brings up in passing during class, in which they are very difficult and time consuming, not to mention graded extremely hard. Do not take her.

This class is very difficult and she just makes it worse. She never makes it clear what you are supposed to do in assignments and is a brutal grader. I learned nothing from her, I had to do data camp and outside videos to pass. The group project is really the main way you can pass this class, so expect to fail a lot of assignments and tests.

This class was the worst thing I have ever taken. As an analytics major you have to take 342 without taking 341 so i came in knowing nothing. She does not want to help you especially since this semester was all online. She has no TA's and only offered one office hour time. I emailed her on multiple occasions without responses. Just try to pass

Jama is pretty intense, and won't spoon feed you answers. This came as a benefit because I still reference the materials from this class in my professional career. She is tough, but fair, and if you apply yourself, you'll be close to intermediate in SQL. Tough professor but a literal lifetime of learning on one class.

Honestly the tags above to choose from are hilarious. She's insanely hard to get a hold of, grades harder than anyone i've ever had, and doesn't explain anything. You basically end up teaching the whole class to yourself.
Absolutely Awful.
Extremely hard class. Start early on EVERYTHING. Learn SQL and meet with your group frequently. Don't take this class casually.
He's an awesome teacher. Genuinely cares about his students and the class is super easy. Test are just like the quizzes and he goes over everything that's on the test the class before.
He is super passionate about what he teaches and wants to make sure that you understand the material. He is very understanding with quizzes and group work.

My favorite sociology class I have taken at UTK. Very chill environment and great discussions in class. Professor Lowery gives really interesting insights into the topics that made the lectures engaging. We each had 2 simple presentations, an online midterm, and a final assignment. He does not take attendance, but sometimes had in-class work.

Dr. Lowery is a great professor. You can tell he truly is passionate about critical theory, and he tries his best to make the complex topics easy to understand. This class is very lecture-heavy and although the lectures can get a little boring, going to class makes the assignments a thousand times easier.

Truly my favorite class I've taken at utk, besides the class being easy, Professor Grady truly cares his students and has very interesting lectures, if I could take all my classes with him for the rest of college I would!! I hope he can read this and know what a great professor he is! GOAT
Professor Lowery was an amazing professor and not a tough grader! He had very interesting lectures and gave plenty of time for assignments.
Definitely recommend! He's interested in what he's teaching but understands it's a 100 level class. One group project and canvas quizzes
Professor Grady's class was very easy, all the answers in the test/quizzes were on the tests themselves.
Such an amazing professor! Would definitely take his class again, it's so easy and interesting.
Grady truly cared about the well-being of students and ensured that his curriculum catered to the benefit of the class. He thoroughly explained all necessary topics and did a fantastic job.

I think that professor lowery was very interesting and I truly enjoyed the class. If you do your work you will do well. Participation is not mandatory but assignments are much easier if you go to class. Topics were deeper and more interesting than other sociology classes because he does not expect everyone to think the same way.
He was a great professor and passionate. Very manageable to get an A. Study hard for the midterm so you don't have a huge percentage to make up. Overall chill class and lecture heavy.

Good professor, he wants students to succeed in his class! Class is lecture heavy but if you pay attention you'll do good in his class. He is very understanding and has open ears. You get in class activities, group projects, and test but they are easy if you pay attention!
Great pressor, super respectful, cares about your grade, super lenient, all the exams are covered in his lectures.

Genuinely such a sweet guy. He wants to help you and the class shouldn't be difficult unless you just don't do your work. Very easy going as long as you communicate with him and he does very thorough exam reviews to ensure everyone does their best. Wants you to succeed!!
Class was always very enjoyable. World politics would normally be something I'd dread but Jake made it very enjoyable.

Jake is a great lecturer, makes every topic interesting and engaging while honoring the reality of global issues. Attendance is graded, every exam is multiple choice and matches study guide verbatim. I attend his class even when I skip others.

Jake was genuinely the best possible professor for my gen-ed requirements for engineering. I cannot recommend taking Jake for this class enough. I enjoyed every lecture and learned more about the world than I ever did in my high school classes.
I took this class online over the summer. Very easy class with very little work. I barely remember what I did. All exams were multiple choice. If you're looking for an easy Volcore, take this class.
live laugh love jake

I took this class as an elective for my major and Mr. Watkins made it so enjoyable. He was so funny to learn from and his lectures were awesome. There are 4 map quizzes and 4 tests (the last one being the final). All the tests are exact like the study guide he provides. He couldn't make this class easier or more enjoyable.Overall, TAKE THIS CLASS!!

Jake was recommended to me and I Can attest that he is the best professor I have had. He is so funny and keeps lectures so interesting. I skipped most classes and would make sure I always went to his. Tests are the study guide word for word and projects are fun and interesting. 100% recommend.
There's a reason this guy has a stellar rating. Easiest elective you can take at UTK probably, and he makes it quite enjoyable. I actually looked forward to going to his class.

Professor Watkins genuinely speaks in riddles but this class was very enjoyable. Would definitely take for a gen ed. He will also randomly start yelling or shouting or doing something crazy during class to grab your attention. He is not a real person which is why his class is so interesting.

Sometimes shows slight political bias in things like Israel-Palestine, but other than that is extremely hilarious and can keep you engaged in his lectures. Tests are not difficult, map quizzes are easy, and other than that, there is no work outside of the final project. Loved this class.
Top 5 Teachers ive ever had. Great energy. Great humor. No homework. Class had engaging lectures and was not difficult at all, but still extremely enjoyable.

Jake was the best professor I have ever had. He is genuinely the funniest guy I know. I wish I could have him for every class. The only grades are map quizzes and exams, but the study guides for the exam are nearly identical, so it is an extremely easy course. Even if attendance was not mandatory, I would still willingly show up every single day.

Hands down the best professor I have had at UT. He's so great I decided to take up a minor in geography and take 2 more classes with him. Hilarious and genuinely cares about getting to know each of his students. You are missing out if you don't take a class with Jake!!!!
basically just one of us

I took this class over a year ago. Right now, my friends are taking him for this class, and I will show up on the occasion to a lecture with them for fun. His lectures are funny, engaging, and very memorable. I believe the fact that I came back speaks to how fun of a class it is. You are missing out if you do not take.

Dr. Brouwers is what you want in a English professor. He tries his best to make the class interesting by touching on relevant topics, his workload is super manageable, he's very understanding towards unexpected events, he grades generously, he accepts late work, and the final is just an essay. He takes attendance and appreciates punctuality.
Dr. Brouwers is a great professor. The assignments are just hard enough, but he explains them thoroughly.

Super kind and chill professor. The in-person class time is mainly there to help you and give instructions. He is very flexible with deadlines when students are struggling. There is no big final exam, just a one page summary of what you learned and how you felt about the course. It is honestly a very relaxing and welcoming class.
Literally such a great English professor. Don't hesitate to take him!!

Dr. Brouwers was genuinely a great professor who cares about his students. There are two major research essays needed but you get the entire semester to work on them and his grading was fair. There were also a bunch of small assignments on the side to easily boost your grade with little effort.

If you're taking 298, take this guy, he's awesome. Legitimately a top 2 professor I had at UT. He wrote my transfer letter of recommendation, and asked me to reach out to see where I ended up after the school year. Amazing dude, and a great grader. If you made errors in your writing, he doesn't kill your grade, rather, gives tips on how to improve

I absolutely loved Professor Brouwers, he is so amazing and I would take his class again and again if I could. The research papers were a lot of work, but he broke it down so that it became easy to complete.

Dr. Brouwers is the best professor I have ever had. He cares about his students and as long as you put in the effort he will make sure you succeed. If you have the opportunity to take his class you are beyond lucky. I simply cannot recommend him enough.

He is passionate and intentional with his teaching. If you are interested in research in the future, definitely take him for this course as he will provide a great foundation for it rather than just giving rubrics and papers. Lectures are very chill.
Cares about his students. If you put in the work you will get a good grade.
Very chill class, requires very little effort most days. He attempts to make class engaging and is very patient with students.

Brouwers was a very good english professor. I honestly hated the class, it was a repeat of stuff I did in high school, but he was an amazing professor. Really helpful and was willing to work with you with grading.

Dr. B is a great professor! He is very understanding and willing to work with you whenever you need help. However, he is a little bit of a tough grader but gives great feedback. He really wants the best for you and to help you grow in this course!!

Dr. Brouwers is an awesome professor! He is very passionate about what he is teaching and his lectures are always very well put together and interesting. He is also very understanding and takes time to go over any issues or questions you may have. Definitely recommend!

If you get the chance to take Professor Brouwers for English 298, do it. He is easily one of the most accessible teachers that I have ever had and stays on top of grading. He truly cares about his student's work and shows a passion for both research and writing. Also, he is understanding and often moves back due dates of important assignments.

Prof Sanders is kind and very helpful! The lectures are honestly very long and a little tedious. If you read the textbook and learn the content as you go, the quizzes and tests aren't very difficult. Your grade is also pretty padded! Great class and professor!!
The goat. Enough said. Prayers for his mom also, RIP.

Dr. Sanders knows the material very well and constantly uses analogies and examples that make difficult processes easy, and sometimes even fun, to understand. He responds to emails very quickly. Some of the exam questions that he uses are pulled word for word from study guides and homework, so as long as you study you will get a good grade.

Dr. Sanders is great. I took him for BCMB 311. He cares for his students, and he knows his stuff. His exams get progressively harder, but he also provides study guides that are very useful. As long as you pay attention in lecture, you'll be okay. One thing is that he takes forever to grade exams, so it's hard to know where you stand sometimes.

Prof Sanders is so nice & chill but his lectures have so much content and he's pretty monotone so it's hard to stay focused sometimes. He only has 3 exams, including the final, so the 2 exams during the semester have so much content & the study guides are so long and wordy so they're really hard to study from.

Dr. Sanders was a decent professor. He was very goofy at times with his analogies and jokes. On the other hand, his lectures are EXTREMELY dense. I struggled to even write down everything for the day. He also had large study guides, three full pages per chapter. All of this made studying for tests difficult. It was hard to know the expectations.

Amazing prof! Basically everything you need to know is in lecture, but still do the textbook readings before the homework to help you. Homework, attendance, and discussion all graded on accuracy and all factor into final grade, along with quizzes, exams, and the final. Very accesible and fun to talk to, definitely recommend taking Prof Sanders

good lecturer, slides are not the greatest for reviewing and studying (would recommend making your own notes from slides since slides information heavy), uses clickers for attendance, homework is online and takes ~5-10 min, good at answering questions, Overall good professor!

Lectures have all the information on them that you need for the exams, and he trys to make subjects fun when he can. You also get to do exam corrections, and the exams are not that bad. Super caring teacher and you can tell he likes to joke around with his students! Highly recommend!!
Prof. Sanders is awesome! He is very understanding. I do wish we had more exam reviews in the class, but the SI helped a lot.

His lectures are very content heavy and didn't make sense some of the times. But you can make a good grade in this class by understanding the broader ideas. You can tell he is passionate about teaching biology!

Dr. Sanders is very knowledgable but this class can be boring at times and incredibly lecture heavy. Clickers are used so attendance is important and quizzes and exams make up a majority of the grade. The lectures have so much information and quizzes cover almost as much as exams which is stressful. Be ready to memorize content quickly.

Nice, funny, and approachable teacher. His lectures were really boring, and his quizzes literally covered SO much material and were probably harder than the exams. His exams are pretty hard too but he offers test corrections for partial credit back. Homework load is easy, but you have to remember that it is due at 10 am not midnight.

Dr. Sanders is such a fun and cool professor. His classes are very engaging and he truly wants his students to succeed. As long as you stay up to date on his notes and look at them outside of and before classes, this class is doable and quite interesting. I would love to take him again!!

Such a reasonable professor allows corrections on tests for credit back and curves according to the most-missed questions. You MUST read the textbook and take notes before lectures - he goes over slides with the assumption that you have.
Professor Yakunin was a great professor and was very understanding about adjusting due dates. Would definitely take again

Konstantin is one of the best lecturers I have ever had. He is very good at explaining topics through math and connecting them to the real world. You can tell that he cares about your success, and he will always respond to emails. Go to class and do the homework he assigns, and you will hardly have to study for exams.

Phenomenal professor- explains everything in a way that both helps you on the tests, quizzes, homeworks, and helps you to understand why the math actually works. Never had a better math professor in my life- the material is well taught and he is very willing to help, offers retakes for every quiz, and grades kindly.

Fantastic teacher- helped me through a difficult subject and challenged students enough to learn, but not so much that students did poorly. Perhaps a little heavy on homework in the beginning of the year, but they always left you well-prepared, even over-prepared, for the tests. Overall a phenomenal teacher!

His lectures were very well organized, and he gave many examples during class. There was a lot of homework, but it was beneficial. His accent is not bad at all. Exams were a little difficult but not too bad if you studied for them.

My experience last semester with Dr. Konstantin was just okay. He rarely did in class examples, it was straight lecture slides followed by some group work time. My biggest issue was the amount of work assigned. Two homeworks per lecture, insanely time-consuming. However, he's a pretty easy grader and allows retakes on his quizzes :)

He is a good teacher, and his accent isn't too terrible. Most of the class is going through example problems which I find really helpful. There's quizzes every week, but you can retake them the following week. The homework can sometimes be really lengthy, but most of the time the tests are a lot easier than the homework problems.

Dr. Yakunin is by far the best professor to learn Calculus III from. Despite there are quite a few homeworks, if you complete them and understand them, the exams are not too challenging. His exams and quizzes can be difficult, just go to office hours early if you want to get an A.

Started out the year rough, went to talk to him at his office hours and my grade immediately spiked. He will give you credit back on tests if you explain your thought process. Quizzes every week but he makes it very clear what they are on, meant to help not hurt your grade. Super nice guy, loves teaching. Overall, would take again if I had to.
Solid overall. Many homework assignments, but this is better than none (coming from experience) as they give relevant practice for the exams. He's very fair and knowledgeable, and always responds.

I had Konstantin for Calculus III, and I loved the class. He goes asks a lot of questions to make sure you are learning as he lectures. I got to know him by going to office hours and it helped my success in the class. There are usually weekly quizzes over the material from the week before, which you can retake.  I would definitely recommend him.

A lot of people hate on him but I loved him. Super nice guy, very approachable. He does assign a LOT of homework and a quiz every week, but all that practice made it so that I barely had to study for the midterms. His tests are harder than average but not THAT hard. Just do the practice problems. Really enjoyed DiffEQ with him!
Konstantin is the goat. All of the exams were multiple choice with partial credit. It is very difficult to fail his class.

A lot of homework was given on a weekly basis, and it was super time consuming/worth minimal credit. The exams were pretty challenging but he threw in a curve or two throughout the semester. Accent isn't too bad. The final was one of the hardest tests I've taken here. All in all, if you have other options look around, but you will pass taking him.

Only reason I gave him 2/5 is because he is generous with partial credit on exams. His English is not 100%, which make his lectures hard to follow. Exams are worth 76% of your grade. The final was horrific and worth 25%. I had more homework in his class than all other classes combined. Expect 2 homework assignments/class. Expect weekly quiz (7%).
Ethan was absolutely awesome! Genuinely cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Don't be afraid to ask him questions and participate in class!
This professor was great! I learned so much and had so much fun. Highly recommend.

Ethan is very kind and cares a lot for his students. His lectures are so engaging and he's super funny. He allows the students to kind of lead the class in whatever direction they're interested in. My only critique is that he doesn't really respond to emails unless you prompt him to look at it in person. 100% recommend!

Ethan is a great professor! He's hilarious and makes sure all of his students are learning and staying engaged. His material is easy to follow through, and his classes are always enjoyable. Just make sure you show up because attendance is mandatory. I would definitely recommend him.
He truly is an amazing guy with a great teaching style. All assignments are online, and he tends to help you on them. I highly recommend taking his class if you get the chance!

I cannot recommend him enough! I knew no ASL coming into this course and he made it so easy and fun to learn. He goes out of his way to make sure students succeed. I will say he NEVER answers email lol but very easy to talk to after class.
Overall for a language class it is pretty easy. There are weekly homeworks and quizzes. 3 test total and the 3 module test serves as the final.

Truly the best ASL teacher that you can get! He made class so much fun and encouraged my class so much. He taught us relevant things we would need to know, not just straight from a PowerPoint. I never felt like I couldn't ask for help or talk to him. I genuinely looked forward to every class.
Great professor, take if able

Ethan is by far one of the funniest people I've ever encountered. Attendance is mandatory but I had no complaints going to his class as a good amount of class time is him storytelling and introducing us to new vocabulary. You def need to be locked in during class and you cant talk, but the hw is easy and not time consuming and all tests are online.

Ethan is an AMAZING professor. Obviously, learning a new language is difficult, but he understands that and doesn't expect us to know everything. Attendance does matter, but he is a little more lenient than others. You will have to pay out of pocket for the online textbook, but it was like $25. I would definitely take his class again.

Ethan is such a great professor. He was so easy to learn from because it was very clear how much he cared about teaching ASL and Deaf Culture. He is also so funny, and every day of class was actually entertaining and I loved going to his class. Would 100000% recommend him!

Ethan is so awesome and caring; he cares about you, and if you need more time on an assignment, he is more than happy to give it to you. He grades the vlogs pretty hard but other than that he super chill grader.

Love this class and this teacher, super hilarious and caring. He is open to repeating anything and is very understanding. Funniest guy I've ever met. Hard grader but it's only because he really cares and wants you to be proficient in the language.
He is fully deaf, so you have to pay attention. He is super funny, and very understanding if you have to miss or you feel like you deserve a different grade on a test or quiz.

Prof Koella was the best public speaking professor I could have asked for! She makes the class super comfortable and is extremely fair. She makes herself very accessible to students and is always willing to help you. She is kind and fun! I would recommend her class to anyone.

Dr. Koella was fantastic! She makes her class fun and interactive, but more than just being enjoyable, all the activities that she does in class really do work! I watched my public speaking skills drastically improve, particularly my confidence, eye contact, and hand gestures. If you can take her, please do!

Dr. Koella really wants students to succeed. As long as you turn in things on time and really try on speeches, you'll get an A. She has a flipped classroom, so lectures are homework videos and then activities in class. The course load is 100% manageable and was a great class for OC credit. She's open and knowledgeable on AI, so be ready to use it.

G👏O👏A👏T👏 Incredibly charismatic, kind, and an amazing professor. I had a nightmare FDOC, and she completely turned it around. This flipped class works perfectly—you learn speeches by practicing, not reading, & why attendance matters. Every class has a purpose, whether you see it at first or not. No busy work, fun, and a breeze. TAKE HER CLASS.

I really liked taking this class from Dr. Koella! She is super nice! I would recommend making a good first impression, and attendance is mandatory. Her expectations going into speeches are set really high, but she gives lots of in class practice and feedback, and she ends up grading very kindly. I would recommend taking her for this course.

Seems like an easy A at first, but it's harder than it looks. There's a lot of busy work and lectures, and many of the final grades were never posted, so it was hard to know where you stood. I had 100s on everything in the gradebook and still ended up with a B because the last half of the assignments weren't entered.

I was so scared for this class because it is all about public speaking and giving speeches, but it turned out to be my favorite class this semester! Dr. Koella is an amazing professor and truly wants to see her students succeed, and really helped me get more comfortable talking for long periods of time in front of the class. Take her class!!!!

Dr. Koella is a fantastic professor! She is beyond caring and will always help you on your speech if you feel you need a little extra guidance. The class is laid out great and she makes you feel very comfortable getting up in front of the class. She is also very funny and has more of a flipped classroom style without in-class lectures!

Dr. Koella is an amazing professor. She cares so much about her students and makes her class actually useful to the real world. She taught my class AI from the ground up, and we were encouraged to use it for our speeches. She is great with feedback and down-to-earth. Do NOT write your whole speech on a notecard and expect an A, though.

Dr. Koella is an incredible and knowledgable professor who makes the course content very managable. With 5 speeches throughout the semester, she makes sure everything is broken into digestible subparts. She has made me a much better speaker and shows genuine care for all of her students. I would take her a million times if I could.

She is very nice, genuinely wants all her students to succeed, and is good at her subject. She is not good at managing the canvas site, be vigilant about making sure all assignments are due on the correct date, and that all assignments are graded accurately, even after the semester ends.

She is a great professor. She is very clear on what she expects and gives great feedback on assignments so you know exactly what she expects from the final draft and speech. She genuinely cares for each of her students and wants to see them do well in her course.
Really good professor! Gave very informative feedback. Biggest thing is she relies too much on AI for writing assignments (weird, I know) which I didn't like but overall really enjoyed the class.

Professor Koella was the best professor I've ever had at UT! She was readily available to meet outside of class for extra help and guidance. She genuinely cares about her students and their success beyond our classroom. Our class had an amazing atmosphere where presenting always felt comfortable.

Dr Koella was by far one of the best professors I have had. Her class is a flipped classroom so there is a manageable amount of work outside of class but she gives great feedback and work on speeches in class! She has a lot of grace and will let you redo speeches if you forget in the middle. I would recommend anyone to take her class! AWESOME PROF!

I've taken physics a couple of times before but it felt like Professor Liu made my overall understanding of physics decrease. He overcomplicated his lectures and would show the complex math leading to different formulas that would otherwise be unnecessary. Thankfully he gave plenty of partial credit on his exams.
Exams are way too difficult for an intro class and he is not good at explaining topics.

(flipped format)
To preface: I missed homework and participation due to sleep deprivation, hence my grade.

Dr. Liu is focused primarily on his students' conceptual understandings. He also grades with this philosophy. I agree with this, but his aversion to confirming lines of thought during instruction is not helpful. This is my only critique.

Mid teacher honestly. He gives a lot of partial credit on exams, even if you just right down formulas. If you just show what you are trying to do with the problem and show you understand it with that, you will get lots of partial credit. Nothing too special about this teacher, but he is not terrible.

As someone that barely understood physics in high school, this class made no sense to me at all. That being said, it was incredibly fairly structured and I could tell that Liu is a nice guy. He balances grades out well, and cares about you doing well in the class. Unfortunately I didn't learn anything, so I can't give a very good rating.

Quantum Mechanics is not an easy topic, but his choice of textbook was solid (Griffith's). His lecture slides are just modified versions of the textbook's material. He does understand the material very well and will explain it in-depth if asked. 40% of the course was homework and the rest was exams, with extra credit quizzes. Fair exam grading.

He has a tendency to read off the slides and his ppts are absolutely terrible. However, he gives A LOT of extra credit that really adds up. For his tests, I would recommend looking over the HW and Pre-labs. Do not depend on them completely though because on the last test, a lot of students struggled since they would memorize the answers.

Physics as a whole at UT is terrible, but 60 bonus points are available and can boost your letter grade by 2 letters. I made like a 66 on the first exam and did okay on the next two and had tons of extra points. You don't have to do well on the exams to do well in the class! Google the homework questions and do them-memorize these for the exams.
Typical physics class at UTK. Do all the extra credit, come to class and do clickers, and do mediocre on tests and you will get an A. Gives so much extra credit it will boost your grade 10%.

Didn't learn a thing about physics, but it was a super easy A. Great for the people who just need the credit, but don't have any interest in the subject. 30% masteringphysics hw, 10% per test, 25% for lab, 15% for final. Up to 10% extra credit for showing up (which I didn't).

Dr. Liu is not the best lecturer in the world, but he posts his PowerPoints, and those are really helpful. Clickers account for an insane amount of extra credit -- with a zero on the final (I didn't take it) I still got an A. Not a great lecturer, but somewhat easy tests and extremely easy class.

I was dreading this class but it was not bad at all.. If you show up to class and attempt clicker questions you can earn up to a 10% bonus on final grade. Oh and tests are only 10% each which is awesome. Homework is 30% and its on mastering physics. Very easy to get a good grade

Chem is hard! But, Dr. McNicholas is a great professor. He is very fast-paced, but as long as you prep before class, you should be okay. He is very accessible and helpful for office hours, and you can tell he really cares about chemistry and his students. Great guy and great teacher!

Chem is hard and it's not his fault. He is passionate about chemistry and wants his students to succeed. He tends to move through slides fast and it's easy to get behind, but he gives ample time for clicker questions and makes sure everyone has had a chance to submit. Go to office hours, pay attention in class, study thoroughly, and you'll do good.

Dr. McNicholas is amazing. If you need to take chemistry, take him! He genuinely wants the best for all of his students. He uses clicker questions for attendance, and is very understanding of outside circumstances. He will do everything to make sure everyone understands the material. Genuinely a fantastic guy, one of the best professors out there.
best chemistry professor in the department

Dr. McNicholas is the best professor ive had. Gen Chem is a tough class but he goes out of his way to interact with students and make content easier to understand. He does personal review sessions over material and goes through every kind of problem you could be tested over. He really shows he cares about his students and want you to do well.
Dr.McNicholas is awesome. He is very understanding and is always open to answer questions during lecture. Probably one of the best chemistry professors for general chem.

I'm naming my first born after Dr. McNickles cause he's the goat. Best professor in the chemistry department at UT by far, though not as hot as Dr. Boyd unfortunately (shout out Dr. Boyd, much love). He hosts personal review sessions before exams that are helpful, and he truly cares about his students. He pushes hw deadlines too. Straight stud.

Dr. McNicholas the best! He explains every problem step by step during lectures. He wants students to ask questions and always asks the class if they understand. He provides lots of resources and practice for exams. He also takes up a lot of his time to hold extra office hours during exam weeks and host personal review sessions. He is also so funny
Take him if you have the chance

I took Professor Puckett for for English 102. All of her classes were very enjoyable and she was incredibly understanding for extensions. There were two main assignments that were worked on throughout the course that weren't too bad as long as they weren't saved for the last minute, which I did.

Mrs. Puckett was my professor for ENGL 102. I highly recommend taking her. There are two major assignments, but they are not bad at all. She really helps guide the class in a very straightforward manner. Additionally, she has a collaborative student-focused classroom that really opens the classroom for open discussion.

Loved this professor! Attendance is based on participation in class discussions, but there is an online post option if you are not comfortable speaking. She is super accommodating and flexible. Grading is kind, and she gives feedback. Definitely recommend.
I took this class for English 101 but it's not an option. She was great, the assignments weren't bad. She's an easy grader and very lenient with extensions.
Many essays in this class, and she had us writing drafts, reflections, and outlines on top of 1500-word essays.

I LOVED Dr. Puckett! Such an amazing teacher with a clear love of English! All of her readings and discussions were so interesting to me, and I never dreaded going to class. She does require participation each class, but gives the option to comment online instead of speaking out in class. Her work was always so easy as well.
Professor Cushman gave really good lecturers, this class covers a lot of material but he managed the time for the material very well. His tests were pretty tough but only gave one assignment a week.

Dr. Cushman's classes are very straightforward. He goes through the guided notes and gives an in-class activity. The quizzes are tough, but most of the exams are light. Not much homework, but I recommend doing the optional homework to study and reviewing the activities.

I've never struggled in a class like I have in this one. I go to his office hours every week, I study harder than I ever have, I show up to every lecture, and I have to drop this class because the exams are so unbelievably difficult. No matter how I prepare, the quizzes and exams make it feel like I didn't even study at all.

Professor Cushman is a really nice guy, and you can tell he cares about students doing well. There's one in-person turn-in assignment a week and a quiz. There are 3 tests, a final, and weekly homework. He is a great lecturer and is always down to help. He does fill in notes that he works through with you, and always writes out what you need to know

I took Honors Calculus 2 with Dr. Cushman, and he was a great professor. He is very knowledgeable of the math and seems to genuinely want to help you succeed. The class' content itself is difficult, and sometimes Dr. Cushman's lectures can be hard to completely understand, but that's mainly due to the nature of the course.

He was a very fun and very good professor. He showed very clear passion for calculus, and gave the class a very deep understanding of integration and series while also giving practice through applications. Easily would take again, he's a wonderful professor.

Idk why there are so many bad reviews for prof Jordan, I really enjoyed having her as a professor. The workload is doable and the tests are not hard. She lets you make your own cheat sheet for every test. People just don't prepare for the tests and wonder why their grade is bad

I would recommend not taking this class online/over zoom. Difficult and tests are very hard. Study early and review throughout the year as it all piles up throughout the semester. Nice professor, but not very helpful. Teaching wise-she just rushes a lot so be prepared with your notes ahead of class time.

Latonya wasn't a good professor at all. The course grade is 80% being divided by 4 exams & the other 20% being the 25+ assignments you do throughout the semester. I failed the first 2 exams & had to drop the class because the best I could do was get a C. The tests are hard and her lectures helped none. Never got less than a B before her either.

I don't understand why the ratings are so low. I will say, don't take it if you don't need to but if you have to, try taking it in person. It is a hard subject by nature, especially for someone who had no prior knowledge. Not a bad prof and definitely passible if you put in the time and effort.

She does an okay job teaching this class, not much explanation, take it in person if possible. She records lectures and attendance isn't mandatory. Accounting is just hard for a lot of students, use the SI whenever studying for exams they are super helpful, she also allows one notes sheet for exams.
Good person but very exam heavy. Expect to get a C in this class. Unless it is needed for credit, pick another course. Too much for a level 200 course.
I'm genuinely at a loss for words.
just change your major.

I wish I would have listened to the reviews about Professor Jordan. I am sure she is a nice person, but I personally had a bad experience with her in office hours. It seems she does not care about her students succeeding. You can study for hours and still flunk a test. Her way of teaching is strange and difficult to learn. Just take in the summer.
As long as you pay attention, the class is easy. You need to study, and she gives you a cheat sheet for every exam. She also posts all of her videos online to rewatch if you are confused.

I am pretty indifferent to Professor Jordan. I don't think she is a bad professor, but if you struggle with this subject, you will need to get outside help. I will say, after dealing with professors who can't communicate to save their lives, I appreciate her over-communication.
Definitely would NOT willingly do this again. Not unbearable, but just not fun in the slightest. Personally did not like her note taking style. Exams are fairly difficult. Only offers o

TAKE THE CLASS BUT GO TO SI/TUTORING. This class sucked! She is the only Acct200 professor and can't teach. She tries to make the lectures easy for people to understand by using slide shows and fill in the blanks but it didn't help me learn. I ended up spending all my time in the SI sessions and with a tutor to maintain my grade.

She's very good and if you go to her lectures you're going to know what you're doing on the exams. Attendance isn't mandatory but it's very worth it to go or at least watch the recorded lectures on canvas before the exams. I would 100% take her class again.
I like her

The class definitely takes time to adjust to, and excel is pretty complex in general, however Professor Smith is very knowledgeable and very good at teaching the material. I also very highly recommend going to his office hours if there's a random function in excel that you can't grasp. He is very nice and willing to answer anything as best he can!

very hard class but certainly manageable if you pay attention to class and study the material while you're learning it. Smith is really good at showing how Excel works and teaching it. but do NOT wait til the last second to do assignments unless you hate yourself

Professor Smith really was not that bad. His workload may seem a lot at first, but it's really essential to understanding the concepts he presents. The workload is not that difficult, but you need to pay attention to the videos. While his exams are open note, I would not suggest waiting until the last minute / not studying.

Mr. Smith was great for an online class. The video notes each week are always under an hour, sometimes under 30 minutes. The homework relates well with the notes and its auto graded with unlimited attempts. The lab is required but can do it person or asynchronously. Three exams plus a cumulative final, but they are all open note.

A lot of work for an online class, but at the end of the day you do learn a lot. Homework is auto graded, you get unlimited attempts, and as long as you get a 90 he'll mark it as a 100. Generally pretty easy to get the week's assignments knocked out the day they're due.
Decent teacher, but makes you watch hours worth of videos every week.

Ok for an excel class, but there's so much homework for an online class. Also very confused on what he actually does, assignments are automatically graded and videos are recordings, so what is he paid to do? Decent professor but I just hated this class

All exams are open-note so it makes it so easy to pass. Only downside is there is lots of assignments.

Also, if you get a 90, he automatically raises it to 100, so no need of stressing.

He's a great online instructor and very helpful! The video walkthroughs for Excel were very detailed and helpful, and I learned a lot from this class. The exams weren't bad; they were open note, and you had an hour to complete them. The final exam was a different format, but if you complete the review beforehand, you'll be well prepared. GO VOLS!
Almost made me want to switch my major due to how time consuming some of the assignments were but the professor is great

Winston is an organized professor. Only complaint is that the final is in a completely different format than all previous exams. If you do the final exam review before you should be fine for it. He keeps your class grade locked until the end of the semester, but a grade calculator is provided. Not a hard class, just a lot of assignments.

The class can be a bit difficult, but all tests are open-note, so it is up to you how detailed you are. The final exam is different than the other exams, but if you pay attention and take notes throughout the semester, you will be fine. He gives practice for every exam, so I never felt very underprepared. Most things build on each other.

I spent at the very least 4 hours on this class per week. The workload was enormous but Smith walks you through each one with a video. Although, on labs and quizzes where there's no walkthrough, his directions are often extremely vague and I need to guess what he's asking for. Overall, the class was pretty easy

Don't listen to anything negative people are saying - I suck at Excel and professor Smith made it so easy. He holds your hand through every concept and anticipates you screwing up and incorporates common issues and fixes into his videos. If you're confused on homework or labs he cites the necessary videos to re-watch within the assignment itself.
SUCH A GREAT TEACHER!! His videos always anticipated my questions, and I learned so much in this class.

Dr. Ansari genuinely cares about his students' success. His final exam closely reflects the review from the last days of class, making preparation fair and helpful. He is approachable, clear, and supportive throughout the course.

Dr. Ansari is a very knowledgeable and straight forward professor. Show up for his classes and study efficiently a couple hours a week and you should have an A. While some of the material is honestly hard, he helps his students make sure that they are able to pass, and he will work with you to keep your grade up.

Dr. Ansari is one of my best professors. He explains concepts very clearly. Although his homework is not mandatory, most quiz problems are taken directly from it. His exams are also very similar to the quizzes.

Dr. Ansari cares a lot about his students' success and the standards he holds them to reflects that. He's not simply a tough grader, but someone who expects coursework to be executed as it was taught. He's quite strict about attendance as well, but primarily because the lecture problems are near exact copies of the weekly quiz problems.

Dr. Ansari is an excellent instructor. His exams closely reflect the quiz problems, and he's generous with partial credit. Honestly, the low ratings seem to come from people who didn't put in the effort-if you study, you'll do well.

It's very hard to understand him due to his accent and how fast he goes through things to get through a whole chapter in a class. He grades very harshly and the final was horrible. He chooses the hardest questions for no reason and makes rude comments to the class about other student's performance.
Very hard to understand, tough grader, doesn't tell you what you did wrong.

I enjoyed his class and could tell he really wanted his students to succeed. He always held office hours and was clear with instructions. There were no homework grades, but he had recommended problems, which were sometimes on the weekly quizzes. I would recommend

Good guy. Hard to understand if not paying full attention. Quizzes can be tough later in the semester as material gets harder, but the exams will be surprisingly easy relative to the problems presented in class. Dude cares a lot about your effort and your performance, but can come across as a bit condescending when pointing out mistakes.

He is very strict on attendance as he wants everyone to do well. Quizzes are every Friday but they are specifically over the homework. Homework is not mandatory but it is very beneficial to do. Exams are not too difficult. Lectures are helpful. He wants you to come to office hours if you need help. He is understanding if you just talk to him.

Very lecture heavy and VERY strict on attendance. Crashed out on my class 3 times because people weren't there. Also very strict on grading and wants you to solve exactly how he would. He tried to take points off for my scratch work on my exam where i was trying to decide the factors for a polynomial?? But does want students to succeed

Very lecture heavy and mandatory attendance but wants students to succeed and makes sure that we always know his office hours. Quiz every Friday, but they are easy and he tells you what is going to be on it.
He was not my main professor in Calculus I, but whenever Dr. Ansari would take the stage, he would teach the material so well that everyone would grasp the concepts so easily.

He means well, but can be a little short when it comes to thorough explanation of content. I took him for M113 and he would not explain things in class because "he didn't have time" and instead would suggest office hours instead. He has a strong accent but it's not impairing in my opinion. Nice guy, cares a lot, but tough grader on a tough class.

Dr. Ansari ensures you understand the material. He is also a super caring guy as well, he has accent but it's very understandable. He did a great job explaining the materials. He uploads all completed notes on canvas as well as test and quiz solutions

Really great professor, tried her hardest to cover all the topics and make sure everyone understands. A lot of people that struggle only do so because whenever she asks if anyone has a question 95% of the time people don't ask questions. Also prepares you very well for the final.
total pile

This is genuinely one of the hardest professors I have ever had. This course is so ridiculously fast paced, and you literally never spend more than a day on a topic, not to mention she talks at lightning speed. Calc 2 is hard, but I feel for me at least she only made it 10x harder.

DR K THE WOMAN YOU ARE!! I can see this class being difficult if you have no calc background, but she genuinely is such an amazing human being. Build a relationship with her!! She's super personable and wants to know her students. Literally so upset I can't take her class again, she rocks.

The worst math professor I've ever had. Weekly quizzes that are much more difficult than the homework and you get no partial credit on them. She always seems to be in a terrible mood when you ask her for any help in class, and is very dismissive. Her lectures are unnecessarily complicated as she makes things seem way more difficult than they are.

Dr. K is an extremely intelligent professor. She often took the time to work directly with her students to ensure that they understood the material on worksheets. However, her lectures move too quickly, and they are presented in a way that requires background knowledge on Calculus. If you haven't taken Calc before, do not take this class.

dr. k, my queen. she provides worksheets on every topic and explains EVERYTHING thoroughly. she is the sweetest human and will sit with you until you aren't confused. she genuinely cares about her students and wants them to do well.

Dr. K cares about her students succeeding and will stay after class to help you. She is very nice but lectures can get confusing and fast paced. Worksheets and quizzes in class every week and hw after every class. This class is difficult so I recommend going to office hours and the math place.

She was not in my main professor in Calculus I, but whenver Dr.K would take the stage, she would teach the material so well that everyone would grasp the concepts so easily. When you ask her questions, she won't make you feel dumb. Worth taking if she teaches a 8:00 am class.

Dr. K is awesome. She is funny and is truly so passionate about what she does. She wants you to think for yourself, so put in the effort and it will pay off! There ARE weekly quizzes but if you do the homework honestly, ask questions, participate in class, and study, you will be fine.
