She is iconic and insanely funny. Her lectures are amazing and exams totally survivable. The class is a lot of work, but reasonably so, and you can tell she is really passionate and genuinely wants us to learn and succeed. One of my favorite profs thus far. Put in the effort, don't fall asleep in class, and you will be just fine!

Dr. K  is very knowledgeable but often explains topics in very technical terms. The examples she works are usually much easier than the homework or exams, which are difficult due to the time limits. Calculators are not allowed, but not necessary. Quizzes at least once a week. While the class is difficult, she does want her students to succeed.

Dr. Kazanova is good professor but she is tough! Calc II is not an easy class at all but with her teaching style and help, she makes the class manageable. If you put enough time into studying, you will do good on her exams. She gives quizzes and worksheets weekly, so be prepared for that! She gives good studying resources so take advantage of them.
Dr. K was very intense but you could tell that she wanted her students to succeed. If you take notes and do the homework it is easy to excel in this class.

Dr. K is okay but defiantly not good/best at teaching matrix. She moves very fast in class so you have to go back and relearn everything after class, because it's kinda hard to take notes and also follow along with what she is saying. And also for some reason she always seemed to be in a very bad mood and took her frustration out on other people.

Pyle makes it extremely difficult to succeed in the class. She provides very few resources for students, her tests are nothing like the review sessions, and she offers no SI. She wastes valuable class time going over unnecessary content. She is way too surface level when explaining material. Her slides do not dive any deeper. Lab is time consuming

I took Micro 210 over the Winter Mini Term which is the the way to go. I am confused by the reviews because Dr Pyle is a great teacher who gave interesting lectures and always took time to answer questions. She is very on top of things, graded quickly, responded to emails, and was very understanding when I needed to move an assignment due date.

I beg you do not take this professor. I have always been a straight-A student, and this class ruined that. Office hours were not beneficial. There are few assignments, so mainly your grade is based on exams. Some people I know did make an A-, so it is doable, but it is a struggle.

This is a DIFFICULT 200-level course. I studied for countless hours outside of class to make the grade I did. You will get the grade you put into this class. Exams are application-based, so make sure to memorize and then ensure you are able to apply it. I enjoyed Dr. Pyle's teaching style.

My advice is to get a tutor immediately. If you come to office hours she will stare at you while making herself a latte with the espresso machine in her office. She skipped slides in lecture that we were still responsible for knowing. Tests are hard and application based. Don't expect any help from her or the TA. Avoid this woman if you can.

Dr. Pyle is a tough professor, and grades start off rough before improving. This is a nursing weed-out class—after the first exams, you'll know if you should drop. I passed Winter Mini (2025) after dropping Fall 2024. Winter Mini is easier,1.5 weeks, online. If taking it in Fall, pair it with physiology, but avoid patho, intro, or anatomy. TRUST ME

Doesn't respond to emails ever even if you send multiple. Tests are made to trick you even if you study a lot. I was in the hospital before the final and asked her to round my grade .8 percent to get a C instead of C- and she couldn't even bother to respond. Don't take if you want a teacher who can communicate

The hardest and WORST professor I've had. Ive never gotten below an A except in this class. I would memorize EVERYTHING on her slides and her tests would be NOTHING like her notes. No curves or extra credit. Reads straight off slides, but has clickers. Goes over only 1/3 of the slides in class and wastes the rest of class with unhelpful discussions

Avoid if possible. Makes exams overly hard for no reason and there are no other assignments to help your grade. I have her PowerPoints memorized and yet I still receive poor grades on the exams. Lectures are required- and does clicker questions. One of the worst teachers I've had at UTK.

This class was incredibly difficult for no reason. All of the questions on the exams were very application based which would have been fine if Dr. Pyle taught this way. She would read off the slides and skip slides with important information that we would later have to teach ourselves. She expects more from her students than she puts into the class

Dr. Pyle was okay.  Her exams are mainly application based, but does not teach her material that way.  You have to do a lot of outside studying in order to be able to understand her lectures and how they relate to the test.  If you are not good at application based questions, do not take her class.

Pyle is a great teacher and makes concepts very easy to understand. This was a online WMT course and it did not feel as hard to do and she helped in every way she could to make sure you were getting what you needed to succeed.

She was overall a good person but I believe that she didn't do a great job at preparing us for exams. Her lectures weren't very content dense which is a bad thing in this class as you need the info. The exams were very hard so you need to study extra to nail the content. Other than that she was a caring person that wanted to help you succeed.

My section of micro lab was lucky enough to have Dr. Pyle as our instructor. She was kind, caring, and incredibly knowledgeable. There were 3 papers, a project, and multiple in-depth exams. However, the lab manual and lectures were enough to prepare us. Do not procrastinate! We would do anywhere from 2-8 experiments a day. It's been my fav course!!

Professor Jarvis is phenomenal and I can't recommend her enough for 203! She goes a bit slower on lectures, but it is for the sake of ingraining concepts that will apply throughout the class and on the exams. She is very nice and always willing to help, and she makes sure you have plenty of resources to help on whatever topic you need.

I had her 8 am and was very worried I'd struggle, because it was said to be a tough class, but she made it not so difficult. There were so many resources. So much practice, notes, textbook, practice quizzes, exam prep days, and office hours every day. She gives very detailed feedback on exams and you can meet 1 on 1 to review your exam.

Professor Jarvis was one of my favorite professors I have had since I have been at UTK. People talk about ACCT 203 being super challenging but she makes it about as easy as it could possibly be. She reinforces everything to make sure you know the material. She cares deeply about her students. Definitely recommend.

Great professor. Semi difficult content if you do not lock in. Exams are worth 25% so it is VERY important to study. However, she is a great resource when it comes to studying and asking questions. She does her best to help students understand the content. Definitely go to office hours. Also, she has lots of resources for studying/understanding!

Prof Jarvis is one of the most genuine and caring professors I've met. Accounting is not easy and she knows that and takes the time in and out of lecture to make sure you understand the material. Follow her study plan, pay attention during lectures, and ask questions and you'll do fine. Prof Jarvis is truly great professor

At first I didn't like Professor Jarvis but she grew on me over time. Each exam is 25% of your grade, so you need to study hard. I got a 74 on the first one then locked in more. Highly recommend you follow her study plan to learn the material outside of class, makes it way easier. Classes are not mandatory except on the day of a couple of projects.
Great teacher
Amazing at explaining topics and has video lectures online for if you have to miss class or just to help study.

Jarvis is a 10/10 professor, but this class is a 0/10. She is super sweet, cares for her students, and provides resources to help you succeed. The problem is the amount of material UTK requires you to get through in a semester is ludicrous. They don't seem to understand that most of us are taking other classes lol. Exams are weighted like crazy too

One of the best Accounting 203 teachers out there. The class is challenging and requires time and effort, but it's rewarding. Professor Jarvis explains everything well and offers help whenever you need it. She prepares you thoroughly for exams and provides plenty of practice material. I would definitely take her! She is awesome!

The course itself is not very explanatory, and there is not a lot of room to actually learn anything useful. I was handed an AI to teach me my assignments. The professor engaged in bad practice by making assignments due on break, sometimes two heavy assignments a week. Some say they had good communication with him; I never did. Geography 411 is bad

He was fine but all of our learning was from online notes, and for someone not familiar with GIS is was difficult to get started. The class wasn't super organized and expectations for assignments tended to change multiple times. I would recommend taking a different professor.

Dr. Sokol graded leniently & was responsive to messages. However, I learned very little; most materials were links to external sites with minimal original content. Although the course is supposed to focus on mapping, most of it was general coding, with geospatial topics only covered in the final weeks. I don't plan to take another class with him.

Good class overall. Easy to take. A little confused in the beginning as there were some materials from a previous semester. Once things got rolling it was super easy. Quizzes each week, some larger activities once every 2-3 weeks, two tests and a final project. Someone else was complaining about the online group project but I thought it was awesome

Amazing professor! I absolutely loved the class. He does a good job blending your typical quizzes with more engaging activities. Since it's online there's not a lot of engagement with the professor, but if you email him he will definitely get back to you quickly. I love him!

I've never had a professor so unprepared for an online class. Much of the material isn't updated and doesn't make sense for an online class. Also, our final is a group project, in an online class. How does that make sense??

Dr. J is the goat I'm telling you. His class for technical and professional writing is very unique. When we walked in, he told us we would be making a board game. For me, it was a daunting task which I didn't know how to start. But he genuinely cares about his students, which makes the difference. Me and my partner were able to make it work out
Really great at econ! Even for honors, wasn't too bad and always gave good lectures!
Good guy that enjoys teaching Econ, and knows what he's talking about. The course was exactly what you'd expect, pretty basic stuff.

ECON207: Always answered emails soon, great lectures (no notes posted online), nothing really bad to say about him at all, straightforward exams (more context than calculation focused), lots of online homework but they are pretty easy.

Dr. Baker is a great professor! Even though this class is all through videos and textbook outside the labs, Dr. Baker still made an effort to make sure he was accessible and involved. Lectures were easy to understand (he wrote the textbook), and exams were fair and easy to ace if you do the review. Definitely your best bet for Econ 201!
He is an amazing professor and actually cares that you understand the material and wrote the textbook himself. The class is a pretty easy A online and the content is very easy to understand.
Easy online class that's mostly busy work. Zoom sessions were every few weeks so you didn't see Dr. Baker much.

Great professor! He made lectures interesting and due to class structure, you have the option to just be online and watch his zoom classes every time and get the clicker points. Need 70% of attendance points to pass (about 7 absences). Quizzes can be difficult but just study the study material and you're good. Would take this course with him again.

He was honestly so great and such a burst of energy on days when i needed it the most. He's very understanding when it comes to late assignments and opening things back up for you. Overall great professor and it's refreshing to see that teachers like him still exist.

He is the best! Made honors econ pretty easy. So kind and wants you to succeed. Tests are worth a lot but his review sessions are super helpful. You have weekly achieve homework that is kind of annoying but not hard at all. Would def recommend, especially for an honors econ course if you need that! I
 would def take him again.

I loved Prof Baker. I took his online Econ 201 classes over the summer term. He is very organized and flexible with his students schedule when it comes to exams. There is work due every day for the summer course, but it is very manageable if you put the work into it. His lectures are good, and he makes examples understandable and fun.

Ken was such a great professor. Each class was mostly just a lecture but as long as you take notes you'll do fine on the exams. Throughout the semester there are 3 exams but you get a one sided sheet typed or handwritten sheet of notes. His exams are only from what he teaches not from the textbook. Would highly recommend!!

Not a huge economics guy so I'll try to take my bias out of this review, with that said, Baker was pretty awesome. Super easy to reach out, grading criteria was clear, and the lectures were decent. Unless you had an iPad or were doing notes on paper, note taking in lectures was difficult with the way the slides were formatted.

Ken was a great professor and actually made economics interesting. The class was very straight forward. He does a few drops for homework quizzes too so that was helpful. He's funny and makes the lectures not so boring.

First off, he wrote the textbook for the class (he couldn't find one he liked), and it was a fun presentation of the material. There are a lot of readings and notes. The content was fairly simple to understand, and Ken did a great job explaining it all to everyone. Just set reminders, and you'll easily pass with a B or higher. Exams are open note

I absolutely love Ken. Great lectures, and he truly cares about teaching us a sometimes boring topic. The class isn't very hard, but the topics are worth understanding. I would absolutely take him if you have the chance.
Super nice professor and glad to be in his final class!
The best teacher. Had a great teaching style and his lectures were genuinely interesting. Tests were not too hard.
One of the greatest to do it. So glad I got to be a part of his last class at UT, and wish this guy the best in retirement!

I enjoyed attending Prof Moon's lectures, often finding his stories and experiences engaging. I felt the content was not incredibly difficult. However, the exams can be a little hard, and they're worth 80% of your grade. Questions on exams often come from examples in class, so if you miss a class, you should rewatch the lecture.
Great guy, great teacher. Makes lectures interesting and will keep you engaged the entire time. His stories are awesome.

Moon is a great professor with lots of real experience stories that help bring the lecture to life. You have 4 test that make up most of your grade, he doesn't give a study guide but the textbook practice quizzes are good. There are lots of test questions from the lecture so go to class and get your clicker points.
Took through zoom, easiest class in haslam

Professor Moon is a great professor! His slides were engaging and comprehensive! He didn't keep you for longer than necessary and he provided good examples to get the material locked into your mind. I wish I would have had him in person!
He makes his test so much harder than they need to be. He just doesn't care it seems. I would avoid him despite what others are saying ngl.
Wonderful guy. He loves his job and cares for his students. If you pay attention in class/take good notes youll do great. I loved him and wish him a happy retirement!

Moons lectures are entertaining, he's very sarcastic so he gets a lot of laughs. His texts are harder than course material though. He loves to throw in questions asking about exact things he said during a given class session, so if you have a poor memory, missed class, or zone out a lot, you're screwed. Had to study a lot harder than I thought
Moon is the best professor I've ever had. Class was easier than highschool classes, and moons funny and nicer than nice. take him for sure.

Seriously one of the best professors at this school. His in class examples are super engaging and helpful. His exams are mostly straight from the lectures so be sure to pay attention. I would definitely take his class again!
best teacher I have had at this university. teaches the material well and is a very caring and genuine guy. Deserves to go in the hall of Fame.

Professor Moon is one of the best professors I have ever had the honor to learn from. He is so hilarious and personable during lectures, making it engaging and actually helps you learn material. He plays music before class and I genuinely look forward to class. Wonderful man and excellent professor!!!
She is a great lecturer and really knows law well. Just be prepared for weekly quizzes which is solely your grade.
I enjoyed this class and the way it was set up. There are weekly quizzes that go over the material discussed in class, which I feel reinforced the information that we learned.

I took this class online, in a mini term and I am so glad he was my professor. Very clearly laid out the grading criteria (4 quizzes) and made sure we knew the quizzes would be based on both lecture and textbook content. He wants you to succeed! I would recommend taking this class in the mini term.

I loved this class. I was nervous at first, but the new format makes it more enjoyable. The only assignments are weekly open-note quizzes that directly reflect the lectures, so good notes matter. I took it online, but I'd probably recommend taking it in person if you can to help stay accountable and focused.

I did not know what to expect when registering for Professor Chyz class, however I am pleased to say that not only did the ratings not do her justice, but she exceeded the expectations. She lectured in a way that genuinely made me interested in the topics we were learning, and I went in with the idea that the law was boring. Great professor!
Professor Chyz made Intro to Business Law engaging. As boring as law can be, she makes it very engaging with stories, cases, etc. She is also very lenient on due dates. 10/10 would recommend!

Professor Chyz is so engaging, and is a great resource to learn this material from. She truly cares for her students and gives many real-life examples for you to really understand and grasp the material. There were no exams, but weekly quizzes that were open-book and open-note. She really wants her students to learn the material and is so kind.

Professor Chyz was AWESOME! She is clearly knowledgeable, and her lectures are excellent. She doesn't use PowerPoints, so be prepared to take notes. Open note and book quizzes once a week. Would recommend!
All you do is take 1 quiz a week for the whole semester. I did the zoom online class and attendance wasn't mandatory but it helped with the quizzes. Definitely recommend taking with this professor.

I took Dr. Chyz's BLW 301 during the summer. The course was primarily graded based on quizzes, which she posted approximately every other day. These quizzes were directly connected to the videos she provided. The course structure was straightforward. I would definitely recommend taking this class during the summer.
She's amazing! I loved her class. Her exams are just like the lectures and she makes it fun and interesting. I almost pursued law school because of her.

Such an amazing professor and lecturer. Everything is based on weekly quizzes that are open note; if you miss a deadline, she reopens everything at the end. Her lectures are fun to listen to and she has the best outfits, highly recommend.

Dr. Chyz gives interesting and practical lectures. The format has changed for this class recently; now, there are weekly quizzes, and this makes up the entirety of your grade. These quizzes are very reasonable and easy as long as you pay attention in class and take some notes.

I believe the format of this class has changed a lot since previous semesters. The only assignment is a weekly quiz that's like 7-9 questions. If you attend class and pay attention, the quizzes aren't too bad. Chyz is a great lecturer and really commands the room, plus she's very nice. Don't be afraid to take this class!

This was the first semester of her new weekly quiz format and it was pretty easy. The only grades are weekly, noncumulative quizzes taken straight from the lecture. Lectures were educational, and made so a business student could understand. I really enjoyed this class.
The class was easy, just sometimes hard to schedule weekly discussions, but you get used to it.

Class was very straightforward. Each week has the same structure with reading materials, a zoom meeting, and a short essay. Professor is there if you need him, but the class is completely asynchronous and pretty self-explanatory. You should succeed as long as you submit assignments on time and give some effort.

Professor Ranft assigned one 20 minute discussion and one paper due Sunday night of each week. As long as you follow his guidelines you will likely get an A on every assignment. It doesn't seem like he takes time to read the papers or watch any of the discussions.

an easy honors class to take. you meet with a group online once a week and then write a 500 word essay. the professor didn't so much as TAs grade everything and he doesn't have much interaction with the class. As long as you follow the prompt you will get a good grade on the essays.
Easy class. Good resources that prepare you for discussions. Grading criteria are not super clear, but still pretty easy. Only discussions, papers, and final projects, so a relatively light load.

Extremely easy online ethics class. Take with people you know if possible! Each week you watch some videos and read some articles and then get on zoom with three other people and discuss it for 20 minutes. Each week there is also a 500 word response to a prompt. Overall not a ton of work for 3 hours of credit.

This was my favorite class. You have multiple videos/articles to read weekly & are responsible for meeting w/ ur discussion group on zoom for 20 min/week to answer questions about the material. U have to write a 500 word essay each week abt the material. The materials so interesting & very informative. It is easy course load and very interesting.

Easy class. Does often feel like you're discussion videos aren't getting watched or essays aren't getting read. I found a correlation between more words and a higher essay score. Informative class. Grading was super fast and everything was graded within a week of submission
Course is fairly simple with lightly graded essays and group discussions each week. Grading takes forever but is consistent.

This class was not hard by any means and passing was very easy. Weekly 500 word essays and 20 minute group convo's over assigned weekly readings did get very boring and repetitive though. Grading also is very slow.

He does not grade any essays, rather he just gives a 94 on every single assignment. This is a good thing until he randomly decides to give out a 90 which subsequently will drop your grade without any feedback or grading criteria. You would think an ethics teacher would actually grade the assignments instead of being lazy.

Clear class organization. You are assigned different articles/videos each week, then must write a 500-word essay and meet with a group on zoom for 20 minutes regarding such material. The class can be somewhat tedious but is an easy A if you actually try. Dr. Ranft does not give feedback on assignments, but he answered my emails in a timely manner.

Professor Ranft ran the online section of BUAD205. The course was clearly laid out and easy to understand on Canvas. Weekly sections including a discussion post and group discussion. Not too difficult and pretty interesting.

This class was super easy, but it is a weekly essay and the topics get so repetitive that you run out of things to write about. Also do not ever expect to hear from this guy or get an email back. I'm basically convinced he doesn't exist.

There are weekly essays (which can get kind of tedious/annoying) and group discussion. He doesn't give any feedback but I've managed to get As in every paper & discussion. For honors, the cutoff for an A is a 94, but, if you try & write well, there should be no issue.
Very knowledgeable of supply chain and must pay attention. His rating should not be this low. Just need to keep up with weekly quizzes and the 3 exams.
Awesome professor, clearly knowledgeable in supply chain and explained his lectures well.
Overall, both professors for this course are bad at teaching. Most people stop showing up. Course itself is designed super poorly and it is hard to know what to study for.

As far as actual teaching goes he's just not that great. If you go to class you won't get much out of it. You are better off not going and just studying the material by yourself (probably will get the same outcome). The course is an easy pass, but built to give you a B (only grades are quizzes and exams so not much relief).

I read these reviews and realized everyone writing them did badly in this class. Why? How? I went to class and took the quizzes. I listened to the etext while working out at the TRECS. Me and my friends studied for the exams and we all got A's! I got my internship based on the stuff we were covering in class. Be prepared and show up!

While he was a nice guy he didn't explain anything that related to the exams, attending class was a joke.  As someone who attended every class and annotated the textbook, there was no way to succeed in the class, do not take this class with him.

You have to read the book to understand what is going on but that is not even helpful on the tests. They expect you to be very knowledgeable about supply chain when this is your first introduction. Lectures are them quizzing you on where you're actually from and throwing around buzzwords, nothing helpful.

He taught with another teacher for buad 331 and they both know a lot about supply chain but were not good at explain it at all. Having to listen to their specific textbook was hard to remain focused and a waste of time. I never passed a test because they were tricky questions with like 6 answer choices. Take the online section
There's a difference between knowing something and being able to teach it. HE CANNOT teach!
Lectures are pointless. Get ready to read the textbook and take weekly quizzes. Only 3 exams and they are very challenging. Try to take anyone else but him.

No teaching actually goes on in this class. Lectures are not required for attendance and are no help for quizzes and exams. Class is mostly just professors throwing around buzzwords. Class should not be hard but is.

Goldsby is a nice guy & has a fun personality, but is not the best professor. He lectures like we should already have a basis level of understanding about SCM- when this course is the intro class to SCM. His tests are nothing like the lectures & the textbook is useless, regardless of him saying otherwise. The TA was more helpful than he was.

I was hopeful for this course because Goldsby seems like a nice guy but it was honestly my least favorite class. There's slides and lectures but the exam questions are nothing like them so you have to teach yourself the textbook (and the textbook is awful). It's hard to get an A in this class because there's few grades and nothing is like the notes

DO NOT RECOMMEND! There is no teaching. His lectures are just him talking as if students should already know and understand everything he is saying. Almost impossible to take notes. Just shows a bunch of graphs. The textbook is no help either. Just simply avoid for your own sake.

Professor Goldsby is clearly knowledgeable about supply chains and his experience is showcased during lectures. Frequently, he'll skip through material quickly or teach in super vague terms, forcing students to go to the textbook to get a better understanding. Unfortunately, the e-text used is not easy to navigate or covered by Inclusive Access.

The class highly associated with the readings. That said, as long as you understand the "key concepts" at the end of each chapter and paid attention to the class slides, you will be prepared for each quiz. The exams can be tough, but there are extra credit opportunities that add points to your final grade. A cheat sheet is permitted for the exams.

Very lecture heavy. She moves very fast so be prepared to keep up. Tests arent that difficult but there are two essay questions at the end and I never felt prepared for the questions she asked. The TAs are hard to understand and my class had a lot of issues with things being graded wrong or too harsh.

Very solid, engaging lectures which is good because attendance (done via qr code form) is a grade. The only downside of the class (in my opinion) is too heavy weighting on 10 question quizzes, but if you do the extra credit you will get a good grade in the class. Somide the TA is the GOAT.
She is a funny professor and if you show up to lecture you will get an A

Holmes was an amazing teacher and really fun. The lectures were usually very engaging and silly. It is very helpful to read the textbook. That's a lot of reading though. Make sure to read and study before quizzes!

Dr Holmes is awesome, and her lectures are engaging and interesting. Definitely no complaints with her. Unfortunately, the Pols102 TAs are awful. I would take this class again, but be prepared to wrestle with your TAs for explanations on grades.

I had Dr. Holmes for POLS 102, she is a great instructor and her lectures are engaging most of the time. The only issues I have are with the discussion sections on Friday's and the TA's. The TA's grade papers and essay's and I think they have a "no one deserves a 100 mentality". Course is good just beware of the TA's.

An amazing professor who genuinely cares that you learn rather than memorize. Quizzes every Friday, and she heavily relies on the TAs, so if you get a good one, you are in luck. The material is super interesting and she teaches it well, but a lot is from the book. Attendance required.

I LOVED Dr. Holmes. This has been my favorite pols class at UTK. While I thought sometimes the lectures were the same, Dr. Holmes always made class fun and I looked forward to this class even though it was later in the day.

Carolyn is genuinely my favorite professor I have taken at UTK. Her lectures are very engaging and funny and you can tell she really cares about what she is teaching and wants all of her students to grasp the material. I will say it is super important to read your textbook and there are pop quizzes almost every week, but still a very good class!!

This is my second class with Dr. Holmes and she is one of my favorite professors. She has such a passion for the course content and is always really invested in the class. While the readings are long (but let's be honest, all poli sci courses have that), she really encourages you to apply them to class discussions. Would 100% take again.

Although Dr. Holmes struggled a bit with being organized, this absolutely does not take away from how great of a professor she is. She knows her African politics, and it was just awesome sitting in lecture listening in. Do the readings she assigns or you will struggle A LOT! She incorporates these readings into class discussion and assignments.

I love Dr. Holmes. She is so passionate about what she teaches and conveys the concepts very easy. I really enjoyed her class and the expertise she brought into the conversations. 10/10 would highly recommend!!

Very passionate about her work. Lectures are great but not posted, so you should show up to class. Pop quizzes are regular, usually on fridays, and can be pretty difficult. Read the readings or you will regret it!! Very understanding and fair.

Dr. Holmes was very passionate about comparative politics! The class consists of 2 exams, a paper, and pop quizzes throughout the semester. I heavily recommend reading the chapters in the textbook before class as they go really well with the lectures. Overall, a great class with a great professor!

His teaching is lecture heavy so take notes. No Homework required. No open note tests (it was open previous sems), so questions were almost exact as in class notes/practice tests. The lab(s) was not well organized and not at all useful. He definitely curved the final class grade. He grades tests very easy, but 4 tests and final is your whole grade.

Good professor with an odd teaching style. I felt his lectures never really connected with the labs we had to do, but they were both useful - just two parallel paths in microwave circuits. He didn't post an official syllabus, but attendance is a HUGE part of your grade. During presentations, make sure you know your circuit specs and why.

A great professor with intentional lessons. He has clear industry experience and conveyed the important topics and had impactful labs to go along. Labs were time consuming but as a hands on learner, very worth it. I would sum the class up to, you get what you give.

The course was difficult material. He has a whiteboard style of teaching, which can make it difficult to review after class if you were unable to get notes down in time. However, he did regularly review material in numerous lectures. He explained the concepts very well and had funny lectures to keep you attentive.
Hes a funny guy and just teaches on equations and not what they are used for. So not really examples more just what equations are.
4 Tests and Final. Do hw to pass
Great guy.
Terrible teacher!

He does not know what he is teaching. Jumping from one topic to the other one. He even cannot design what he teaches. He just know some theory which learnt when he was a student and that's it. I don't recommend him.
Dr. Ye is super understanding and really goes out of her way to help you grasp the material.

FINC455 is lecture-heavy and time-consuming. You need to pass it to graduate. I found this not easy if not put effort into it. But I learned quite a lot. Dr. Ye is very kind, probably better than many other utk professors I have seen. I showed up a few times during her office hours, and she was always helpful and clear on the topics.

Quite possibly the worst professor I have had at UTK. Does not show any enthusiasm to teach or care for student success. Reads purely from the endless amounts of slides. Homework is tedious, useless, and unnecessary. She will tell you the class before when projects are due. Canvas page is enough to give anyone an aneurysm.

Pick someone else if you can. This class is so unorganized and is so time consuming. I couldn't tell you one thing I learned only in this class that I didn't learn in 420 or 425. Professor only made a bad class worse. If you read the practice exams you will be fine, but other than that the class is pointless.

This was Dr. Ye's first semester at UTK. FINC455 is boring and lecture-heavy, but she is better than other professors for 455. She uses Excel for all examples even though we can't use it on exams. The exams weren't too bad if you use the practice exams to study. Homeworks take a long time but they weren't due till the end of the semester.
Knowledgeable of supply chain, just must pay attention. Keep up with quizzes every week.
Great professor, very knowledgable.

His notes were very helpful as they basically answered the homeworks. The homeworks basically answered the exams/quizzes. The labs were very easy. He is very respectful to all students in/out of class. He also gives lots of real life examples and gives tips on how to be a good engineer in your future electrical career. Would take any class with him

To be honest, Brian's teaching style just did not work for me personally. But I'm still giving him at least a 4/5 because he is probably the kindest professor at this school and I know he worked great for sooo many other students. I also enjoyed all of his life advice. I wish more people were like Brian. I teared up during a few of his lectures.
The class overall can be challenging, but he gives so many resources to help students succeed. He is also very accessible outside the online class, and always willing to help.
Lectures are half content and half his life story. And there is so much homework.
Tries too hard to be funny, lectures could easily be 30 minutes but are instead stretched out for 75.
Brian is a great guy and truly cares about his students. This class is homework heavy, but I felt very prepared by the time the exams came around!
He is awesome, could not recommend enough. Gives ample resources and truly cares about students success. However, if you are easily overstimulated don't take it there's a lot going on!
This class was fully online. Basically, a YouTube course and the live streams let you get extra credit. The exams were pretty easy, and Brian does a great job of teaching.

Brian is the best! He makes an effort to help you in every way he can. If you are taking stats, I would not take anyone else. GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS! He offers them online, and I went almost every time; he walks through homework, data analytics, and general examples. He wants you to succeed. If you study and pay attention, you should do great!

I have had Brian twice now and would take him for every class if I could. He is very passionate about stats and is very accessible. I also learned better by doing projects instead of tests, and it was way less pressure. Definitely take him if you can!

This is my second time having Brian Stevens (BAS320 also), and the reason I chose to go with him again is because he knows this is a hard program (R) , especially for non-coding wizards like myself. However, he is fair and the way he explains the concepts makes sense! He really cares about the subject being taught and his students! Great professor!

He makes class interesting and fun by making an effort to acts like friends with students rather than being above us. He is very easy to reach, kind, a nice grader, and a great professor! Take his class if you can!!

He's a good teacher for the most part, but his class is so ADHD and off topic. While he tries to make it so he can help people pay attention, I thought it was so hard to pay attention and honestly did not enjoy going to this class.

Brian Stevens is one of the best professors I ever had! He encourages memory retention and understanding of material through fun and engaging lectures. Furthermore, he deeply cares about the well-being of his students and is frequently accessible outside of class. I would encourage anyone who needs a Statistics course to take his class.

all online course with youtube live lectures. Great professor who is passionate about statistics but classes are very overstimulating. He makes class enjoyable but only a third of class discusses statistics, over 2/3 is about random topics. Test prep is great and has so many helpful videos.

Not only is he arguably the best STAT professor, but also one of the best professors period. The way he teaches his online classes is the best I've seen an online class organized and structured. TAKE HIM IF YOU CAN!
I loved his lectures, very engaging and funny! All of his exams were easy and he clearly cares about his students. One of my favorite professors.

Dr. Brewton was funny. He tends to ramble in lectures, and the tests typically cover the textbook material. He does clicker questions each class, but the code is the same all year. He's funny and clearly cares deeply about his topic and is excited to share his knowledge. Overall, I enjoyed his class and would recommend it.

Dr. Brewton is very passionate about his work. He loves to get the class engaged. He uses clicker points so if you attend lecture its easy points. Exams are usually not based off his lectures but he posts exam review questions that are very helpful and would help me to get 80% on exams. There are other online quizzes and lab grades that help!

He tends to ramble during lecture. The tests are 95% over the textbook readings. The lab portion is very elementary and feels like stuff that I would've done in middle school science class, so it can get boring really fast. He does clicker questions during the lecture so missing class will drop your grade pretty quickly. Overall good & easy!

He is pretty good. Biology 102 is relatively easy, so the exams are a breeze. He can ramble in lecture and uses clickers. You can stay home and just watch the clicker/do the questions and you will be just fine. Little homework so the exam grades matter a lot.

Knowledgeable. Coursework was quite manageable. Labs were helpful in learning the content. He did sometimes have an attitude when people would ask questions or when responding to emails, but he's probably tired of having to answer questions that could probably be answered by looking at canvas. Not very lenient with technical issues/other mistakes.
Dr. Brewton was very knowledgeable about Biology. He can be condescending to some students, while other students not so much. If you study hard before exams, you will do fine.

He does not really care what is going on with your life outside of class. A couple times he has taken off points for people on clicker questions because they had a fire drill and couldn't bring their laptops with them. His lectures are basically useless and when preparing for the tests, use the textbook and don't even bother with anything else.

This guy will not answer any of your questions well, and instead talk about something completely unrelated. Work isn't very well outlined and is on so many different platforms its hard to keep up. Cheating in this class definitely better than in person bio, but if you want to actually learn something useful, this is not the class.

Honestly a rude guy. Did the point solutions way too fast, so if you missed one “there will be other opportunities”. Material and class was overall easy though, so I can look past his attitude. Online option was a great choice.

He is incredibly inconsiderate of his students. Rambles, makes strange political remarks and walks around and makes fun of specific people in class. No clear grading criteria/can't view your grades. Genuinely an easy course content and test wise but his personality and teaching style was unbearable.

Have you ever wanted to take a class where you spend more time trying to figure out what you need to study for the test over actually studying material for the tests? If that sounds like fun, take from professor Brewton! Literally take from anyone else, hated every second of this class. The labs are super easy though, only positive of this class.

Brewton is nice and funny but I didn't enjoy the class. I got a 50 on a lab because it did not go through on his side for some reason but I had proof I submitted it on my side and still was only given half credit. It dropped my grade to a B and I wasn't able to get it back up to an A unfortunately. The class isn't hard but it can be time consuming.
A very good class, but I wish it emphasized hands-on learning even more. Labs are useful and pretty easy tho. Professor Brewton is a very nice person. Would recommend.
Sometimes the lab website would not work, and it took him a while to correct my grade. Other than that, he is fine. Very average professor.

Difficult exams but he taught the material well. Coming from someone who usually just plugs my class slides into ChatGPT and tells it to teach me the information, I actually never had to do that for this class. You NEED to come to class tho. Def the only reason I ended with an A. Besides the third exam, I actually kinda enjoyed this class ngl

We had four tests throughout and the final exam was optional (5 total tests drop the lowest grade). Dr. Hanson was an amazing lecturer who made the content so interesting! Homework assignments were long but helped you understand. He is very accessible. Would absolutely take again. Tests were challenging so STUDY and READ the textbook.
Dr. Hanson is a good teacher if you put forth the effort. He is very sarcastic and funny once you get to know him, but he will help you as best he can.

This class is challenging. The tests are much harder than the homework's and in class questions you complete. However, if you show up to class and focus then it is much easier. Prof. Hanson did a good job explaining all of the concepts.

He is a really good teacher and is passionate about what he does. I found his lectures interesting and easy to understand. As long as you pay attention to what he is saying and take notes the class isn't bad at all. The exams make up a large part of you grade but he gives a lot of extra credit throughout. So overall he was a good teacher.

Professor Hanson comes off strict when you first meet him, but eases up after the first class. Uses clickers to take attendance. The clicker questions are from old exams. He doesn't allow laptops in class, so if that's a dealbreaker, definitely do not take his class. Makes a hard class like econ interesting and is definitely passionate about it.
Professor Hanson is super sweet and a really good professor. Make sure you study, study, study, because the tests are difficult.

He walks around when he lectures, and it's distracting. The stuff we talk about in class is not entirely what is on the exam. It's a more complicated version of what he talks about. IDK, econ just doesn't make sense to me, and so maybe it was just me.

I saw many positive reviews of Prof. Hanson, but I did not have a good experience. His lectures felt long and hard to stay focused. Clickers in every class. I studied a lot for exams and still scored lower than expected. There are 5 exams, and they felt very close together. He's so into econ he thinks hard concepts are easy. Has weird class rules.

Econ is a difficult class, but Dr. Hanson is a great professor to take it for. Seems strict at first, and no laptops are allowed, but he's really just honest about the course difficulty and is a super chill guy. Super accessible outside of class and encourages you to get help if you need it. Looking forward to taking his classes in the future!

Hanson was great. He was easy to access outside of class and cares about his students understanding or doing well. He has good lectures and the homework is helpful. At the beginning of the semester he acts like a strict professor but he really is not, he does not like laptops so you have to take notes on an IPad or notebook.

Very good professor. Class is always a lecture, but he breaks it down in an understandable way. He uses point solutions for attendance and requires that you take notes by hand. Would def take again if I could made the class easier than it should of been.
Very caring and passionate professor. He knows his stuff and was always happy to help! I do not think he made the class difficult for me, it was more the content.
Very organized. You always know what is due and when. The concepts aren't that difficult if you do the PCP and HW. LOVE HIM.
great lecturer, explains the material so well. unfortunately his self-made exams are tricky and will make you second guess yourself

I found that this class was unnecessarily hard and overcomplicates simple lecture material. All exams/assignments are written and all exams are proctored essays to be done in class. Exams also had nothing to do with lecture, they were only based on primary sources. The primary source was also not revealed until the exam! Take anyone else tbh
Seminar-based class, but a nice professor. Hard grader except for the final!

Tbh I was super excited for this class to be deeply let down. We sit in a circle and wait for someone to talk for 75 mins about nothing of substance. Toughest grader I've ever had, expects us to all be PhD level thinkers. Nice guy but thinks the world revolves around his class. Get ready to dedicate about 30 hours of reading a week just to get by.

As as history major this class was a snooze fest. Every single class for the entirety is just a conversation You conversate about what you found interesting or whatever in the 30+ page reading You barley learn anything new or insightful because the entire class is going over what you already knew. Harsh grader. Just avoid unless youre a hist major.

One of the hardest professors I've taken. He teaches solely off of quotes from the primary sources, and the class in general is more like a philosophy class. Discussions were used as quiz times instead of being used to talk about the sources he assigned for reading every week. Do not take this course if he is the professor.

he made us write lengthy papers that had nothing to do with the material we learned in class even though we had to go to the lecture everyday so we could do the discussion posts. we would have pop quizzes during discussions which took the meaning out of discussion which is to help understand what we learned. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!

He makes sure that you have to show up to lectures by requiring a discussion post after each one. There are also readings that you are supposed to do before each class and for discussion class. The lectures aren't all that interesting and his slideshows are mainly quotes and pictures.

Not clear about grading criteria whatsoever, you MUST go to lecture. Very anal about grades and he just overall was not a very caring teacher. Hard to reach, would just tell me to email my TA if I had a question.

There is so much unnecessary busy work for this class. After each lecture you have to write a reflection and submit it to canvas and the TA's don't go easy on you. The grading for this class is super tough for an intro class. There is a discussion section every week that is pretty much pointless and a waste of time. Midterm and Final are writing.

DO NOT take the class if you are expecting a normal history class-if you were hoping learn about important events in time, ancient civilizations, cities, wars you WILL BE DISAPPOINTED. He does not teach like any history teacher I've ever had-90% of the slides are quotes and pictures. More PHILOSOPHY than History.I was completely mislead signing up.

Took this class async, and it was very nicely set up. Lectures were interesting. Tons of reading, but most of it was interesting. The class had a large emphasis on religion. Discussion groups were probably the hardest due to having very specific questions, but overall a pretty good experience.

Usually love history but this guy made me hate it. Gives absurd amount of reading and homework. Super long lectures with discussions post so you have to do it. LOTS of writing too. The material of this class is super boring too. I strongly recommend not taking Latham. If you do get ready for a semester of misery because I hated this class.

It was an online class so I did not interact with him much (more so with the TA), but his lectures were thorough and he was funny and nice. There were discussion posts and then three primary source assignments and the midterm/final so not too much work. Make sure you read and look at the sources and participate in the meetings and you'll be fine.

Taking this class virtually is weird. The only interaction with Dr. Latham is through lectures he prerecords. The only point of contact is through the TA. His assignments are centered around reading. The readings usually are about 80-110 pages a week, so it can be very overbearing. You must do the readings to understand what is going on!!

Dr. Latham is one of my all-time favorite professors. He is very approachable and his style of lecture is both informative and entertaining. I was initially worried by some of the other feedback left here, but Dr. Latham is really a great professor. Yes, he expects you to read, and yes there are papers (this is college after all).

The best professor you can take. I hope HQ knows what an impact he makes on his students. Great lectures and you can tell he cares and wants to help his students. Would definitely recommend any class of his.

I loved him! He is so personable and understanding of students lives outside of class. He knows what he's talking about and loves to teach, will always take his class again! Only thing I will say, there are not many assignments, so do the few well.

Easiest class and best teacher ever. You can never attend class and get an 80% for attendance. Super valuable work that actually relates to real life but he is a super easy grader. Great guy, great class!

HQ is rad.  He teaches a lot differently than most professors, and it works really well for a business class.  Engaging lectures and not much homework.  He pulls a lot from personal experience, and he really focuses on students getting experience with entrepreneurship in the real world.  W prof.

He is so much fun! Extremely personable and easy to like. The class itself was great and i learned alot of entrepreneurship in general. He is not that great about answering emails, but he stays busy. Very kind and truly wants the best for you.

By far the best professor I've ever had. I intentionally seek out his classes because he's so great. It's clear he cares about his students and wants all of them to do well. Very understanding and willing to help you always

This is 20 straight 5/5 ratings for HQ, so... should probably listen to that. With that said, he cares so much about what he's teaching and is a total open book to the class - always wants to know what's going on in our lives. Slow to grade, but more than worth it.

HQ is one of the best professors at UTK in my opinion. He cares so incredibly deeply about his students success, he is open and honest and unapologetically himself, and most importantly he offers an invaluable perspective on entrepreneurship because he himself is an entrepreneur through and through. I have gained so much from him, can't say enough!

HQ was by far the best professor I have had in The Haslam College of Business. Not only does he have a lot of knowledge on the topic of entrepreneurship, but more importantly he understands students and seems to actually care for us, unlike many professors. He ackownledges that we are humans and have lives outside of school. Highly recommend him!

Great professor, goes above and beyond. HQ is very understanding and is very knowledgeable. His class isn't mandatory, but if you don't show up you do have to watch a recording of the lecture. You have to present twice in his class but it is only for a few minutes. There isn't any HW in the class and it is a pretty straight forward and easy class!

Don't leave many reviews on here but thought he definitely deserved one. I'm about half way through the course so far and have loved it. Its and 8am and I'm not really a morning person but the vibes are great and he is an extremely positive person. You can tell he genuinely wants everyone to pass and succeed. 10/10 recommend would take again.

One of my favorite professors! We're only halfway through the semester but I wanted to rate him now so people planning their classes could hear this. I had him as my professor of an 8am class but he makes it worth going. He always has a great attitude and is willing to talk to you and help you. He makes the class fun and interesting.

HQ is hands down how I think every professor should be. Not only is the content interesting and useful but HQ makes his lectures fun. The fact that this class was online, had no exams, no overbearing amounts of homework, and was a class that I probably have taken away the most from, should say something about how teaching should be. Thank you, HQ!

I never have rated any professors but wanted to take the time to rate HQ. He is by far the most engaging, caring, funny professor I have ever had. You genuinely want to show up for class and he teaches the insides and out of entrepreneurship from conceptual ideas to his real life experiences. I am upset there isn't another course with HQ past this.

This dude is the GOAT. Literally makes class so enjoyable and allows you to actually learn about entrepreneurship. He is very passionate and knows what he's talking about. One of the funniest dudes ever. Take the class, you won't regret it. This man is awesome.
I loved her! Such an easy class, no proctored exams and attendance was not mandatory. She was great and clearly passionate about her job!

This is the easiest class I have ever taken. I took this class as an in person class but barely ever showed up and still made an A. When I went to class it was super easy and she is the nicest teacher you will ever have.

The sweetest professor at this university. She is so passionate about her class and she tries so hard to be inclusive to everyone. Honestly, you could never go to her class and get a 100 in the course. She doesn't take attendance, and eventually many people stopped coming. Such an easy A. TAKE HER!!

I had her for HDFS 210, and she was amazing. She's super kind and funny, and the class was overall very interesting but easy. There is a textbook, but I never had to read it; I just did the learning curves, paid attention in class, and was just fine!

TAKE HER CLASS!!! I could go on for pages about her - she is one of my all time favorite professors. She truly wants to get to know you and take the time to help you. There are 3 Quizzams all open note and then a research proposal at the end but with baby step assignments leading up to it. She is amazing!!!!!

Dr. Johnson is amazing! CFS 395 is so insightful to the world of research and her lectures are great! She really cares to get to know her students, go to her office hours and talk in class- you'll do great! There are 4 Quizzams and one big research proposal project that is broken down into many parts of the semester. She is so understanding too!!

Dr. Johnson is one of the most caring professors I have ever had!!! She tries her best to keep you engaged during lecture, and she is always very understanding of any questions. She always pushes her office hours & I recommend going if you need help with any assignments, especially the papers because she gives amazing feedback! TAKE HER!!!!!

Dr. Johnson is the absolute best professor I've ever had in my time at UT. I would take her for every class if I could. She is one of the most caring individuals I've ever met. I have also learned so much from every course I've taken from her. Every time I take her I am reminded of how much I love to learn.

Professor Johnson is extremely engaging during lecture! Esp when she knows the material may not the most interesting, she tries her hardest to make it something we'd want to learn. She does grade her papers pretty tough, but if you go to office hours you'll be golden! Wish I could take her again!

Dr. Johnson is a great professor. She is caring, nice, and knowledgeable. She uploads outline presentations for class, and then the students fill in their own presentations from the information she provides in her lectures. There is also one group project around the beginning of the semester and one paper due at the end of the semester.

She is my favorite teacher I have ever had. Her lectures are interesting and fun. Also, she genuinely cares about her students and wants you to do well in the class. She isn't easy, but she will help you outside of class if you need it! Would absolutely take again!

Took this class as hybrid (online & in-class) & I preferred this, but you have to keep up with online lectures (only up for 2 days). Tests are pretty easy, presentation went bad bc of my partner (tough grader on this), participation is major! She's one of the sweetest teachers & knows her stuff. I recommend hybrid version vs. all in-class.

Dr. Johnson is awesome. You should try and listen to all the online lectures and attend all classes. She does excuse a couple absences, but to get an A read the chapters and go to class. She is so helpful and willing to lend a helping hand if you are struggling. Fun class. Interesting lectures. She does take presentations seriously, though.

She is very enthusiastic, which makes listening to her lectures painless. She genuinely wants people to learn and will slow down if you ask. It sometimes seems likes she is making an easy subject more difficult that necessary to prove herself, but her tests are still very easy. Most questions come straight from the notes.
Dr. Johnson's tests are easy but she grades papers super hard! Also plays favorites a lot esp in determining who she will write letters of recc for!
Very knowledgable on the lecture topics with helpful supplemental resources during the modules, quick to grade assignments.

Professor Buehler is awesome! This is my second class I'm taking with him, and have loved both of his courses. He is incredibly passionate about the topics he teaches and his lectures are fun and engaging!
Very interesting course.
He was great, highly recommend.
engaging and knowledgeable instructor, and if you follow his guidelines and put in effort, you'll do well in the course.
I think this was a great class, it is a lot of work but easy to communicate with him.

I would highly recommend taking a class from Professor Buehler. He is very knowledgeable on Middle Eastern studies and his lectures are very informative. As far as assignments go, as long as you meet his criteria and put forth effort, you should be successful in the course.

Professor Buehler is one of the best professors I have had here at UTK. He is a stickler for how he wants things done, but as long as you read the syllabus, he lays out exactly what he wants and how to do it! Definitely take it if you want to actually learn the material well!

As an International Affairs concentration, this was one of the most worthwhile classes I took at UT. The syllabus clearly lays out expectations, deadlines and grading. There is a fair amount of reading, but the assignments connect well to the readings, making the workload manageable. Having one due date for assignment made the course very flexible.
Reading heavy with good lectures that help you understand materials. No tests and one due date.
The class has a lot of work, but the modules are organized well and his instructions are clear.
Pretty simple, very outgoing and peppy, pretty nice, not super organized homework or classwork, but extremely loosely graded, so it weighs out.
Pro 3 credits in 2 weeks Con a lot of work to do it 2 weeks.
Very helpful professor, everything was clear and work was manageable. He supplies a timeline to pace the workload. Would take again.
Clearly very passionate abt the coursework, deadlines are clear and manageable!

This instructor has no idea how to teach or structurer a class. Explanations are vague with little clarity and no direct answers  to questions. Lectures are boring, easy class, most of the material can be learned more effectively through  self-study.

Lectures were not particularly interesting, and often times it just sounded like words were being said. Exam and quiz problems are pulled directly from the homeworks and she told you exactly what problems would be on the exams.
Started working out exam questions on the board during exams. I only went on lab days. Very easy. Good professor, okay lecturer, boring material. Responded to my emails months late.

not a great lecturer, hard to concentrate, nicest woman in the world though, asked her about a question mid exam and she told me the answer, labs can be unclear for grading requirements, hws are easy, exams are straightforward, im getting an A and played Clash Royale everyday in her class
The class itself is pretty mundane, but as long as you review the homework and slides you should pass fairly easily

Dr. Taousser is a boring teacher, but she gets through the material. I often skipped lectures, as they were early and boring. For the exams, just study the homework, and you will be fine. Be aware of quizzes, however. She claimed all 3 would be announced, but the first was not. Overall, pretty easy class. Just boring.

A pretty laid back class. If you just show up and pay attention to her announcements it should be an easy A. She covers necessary topics just fine, but success does not rely on the lectures. Decent overall.

This class is mostly attendance optional. I say mostly because part of your grade is "announced quizzes" except they aren't announced, she just gives them whenever she feels like it. As far as the lectures go, I just sat in the back and watched Dexter and ate breakfast. Most of the assignments are on canvas. Go to class for the quizzes.

I went to 2 lectures the whole semester and got an A. I'm not a genius either, the median scores on the midterm and final were 97.5 and 94, respectively. Every other assignment is given online. You can comfortably sleep in during the 8am time slot this class is offered.

For this class, you honestly don't particularly need to pay attention to the lectures to succeed. Though if you do, you will find them a bit boring albeit fairly helpful. Exams were very similar to the homework, so it's easy to know what you need to study. She also gives many essential formulas on exams, so you don't need to memorize everything.

This class is easy, but that was the only saving grace for everyone. Dr. Taousser is a bad lecturer, boring and more than half skipped. She doesn't respond to emails consistently, and she isn't very tech savvy despite this literally being a circuits class. She gives out little information too, we found out when our midterm was 2 weeks in advance.

Dr. Taousser's class wasn't the most difficult class, but the numerous worked examples she provides ensures your never left behind. While none of the material was very difficult, some assignments near the end for Fourier Transforms was quite time consuming.

Really boring lectures that you don't have to go to or listen to. Online quizzes and homework. Exams in person. Office hours worth going to, she is very nice and helpful. After going to this class, I don't understand anything about ECE-313 or its contents, but my grade in the class is alright.

Lowkey awful lectures. Not engaging at all, but considering I learned practically nothing it wasn't the hardest class in the world. Homeworks and multiple-choice quizzes were all online, while the Exams were in-person.
Lectures were at 8 am and she didn't take attendance, so people skipped a lot. Not the easiest to understand, but the material was okay. It's ECE but it's a statistics class in disguise.

He cares about how well students do and encourages them to seek help if needed. His lectures are easy to follow and understand and many quiz and test questions are extremely similar to ones done in class.

Tests are majority of the grade and the lectures are alright but not very helpful in teaching information. I was heavily relying on The Organic Chemistry Tutor. He started the class saying it would be a miracle most of us pass so not the most inspiring professor, I liked his math shirts though.

Class is difficult if you haven't taken a calculus class before, but if you do the homework and pay attention in lecture you will be fine. The tests and quizzes were difficult, but it's never a surprise of what's on it.

Overall, very good professor. Teaches well, teaches clearly, keeps your attention well. Is always there if you need help. Always reminds of upcoming deadlines. Likes difficult questions on tests/quizzes. Not a big fan of curves. Big fan of using absolutely every minute of class to teach. Very down-to-earth person overall and worth taking.

Prof. Sukanek is very good at teaching math, he makes calculus concepts very clear and easy to understand and always reminds students of upcoming homework, quizzes, and class expectations, so nothing ever takes you by surprise. Peer reviews are 4% of the grade, and 15% of in-class activity points are dropped, which really helps boost your grades.

I have to disagree with these reviews, Mr. Sukanek was not the best calc prof. Kinda condescending. Quiz content was far more difficult than examples in lecture. Didn't like his lecture style, he'd give us a problem and let us loose without explaining a thing. I had to teach myself everything after class. Highlight of the class were the TAs.
Dr. Sukanek is a great instructor! He is very understanding and super approachable. You can tell he wants all of his students to succeed in his class.

I took Sukanek for math 131 and math 132. He was awesome. He explains concepts very well and he cares if you are learning and when we do in-class practice problems, he will come around and make sure your table understands. He also makes outlines for each exam/quiz telling you what you need to know.
Professor Sukanek is the goat.

One of the BEST math teachers I have ever had. He helped me understand everything and he explained everything so well. He really wants everyone to pass and he gives clear instructions and examples of what's on the exams. Does a lot of examples which helps so much and has AMAZING note taking guides. 100% would recommend.
Sukanek is great, he explains topics very well and makes it easy to understand. It only gets hard when he lets the TA's teach... He is open for questions, and is very kind.

His classes made Calc so easy to understand. He answers emails really quickly. If you aren't able to make it to class, he as a zoom class during his lecture. The homework is easy if you take notes during the lecture. He also has a note taking guide you can use for notes. Loved this class and this instructor!

I had Math 132 at 8am three days a week with Mr. Sukanek, and I dreaded it every day. He holds a zoom during his lectures, which I often end up doing because being in his class puts me in a bad mood. He is super condescending and whiney and will talk to you like you have no knowledge of math.
I love Mr. Sukanek, he made calc feel easy and doable. His guided notes were super helpful and although there's a lot of homework, it works as really good practice.
shows a problem and tells you to figure it out with your group with out any other explanation. then you leave

For HART 2779. Dr. Chesney as a professor is a post doc fellow. His class is challenging, only in the sense that you have to put up with interesting views. Book heavy, unclear grading rubrics. 3 confusing midterm tests. He's a bit unaware of his privileges, thinks he's a very “based” anarchist.
honestly a great prof, classes are very intimate and conversational and if you get to know him you will learn alot about architecture and do well in the class.

I'm kind of shocked Dr. Chesney has a low rating. I found him to be a great professor. You should be able to succeed in his class if you engage with the class and the content and show an interest. My personal qualm with him is that his organization can be somewhat loose, and will often send you the readings a few days prior

Not sure why the review below says anyone who gets below a B is lying since I got a C in the course despite showing up to almost every class and turning in every assignment on time. This course is known for being one of the most grade deflated in all of Vanderbilt.
Anyone on here saying they got lower than a B is LYING. He's just opinionated so some students get mad. very intelligent teacher
very smart guy, learned a lot from him
Grading metrics very weird, even if u get an A on all assignments, one B can result in an overall grade of a B. Course average sits around a C to a C+.
Sent out a spiteful email bc we were complaining that 2/3 of the class had B- or below.
i cry everyday, i cannot go to law school now

Chesney was irregular, but clearly an intelligent guy with lots of insights. Lectures & approach to studying the city were engaging but sometimes felt unorganized. 3 exams + 2 movies + participation for the grade. Split the class 50% A's and 50% B's. I enjoyed the class, but wouldn't recommend for those looking for an easy humanities class.
terrible professor...nothing else needs to be said

The lowest grade in this class he gave out was a B-. It's honestly not hard if you show up to class, and he provided about 5 extra credit opportunities throughout the class! The hate he's gotten is so not deserved, and I hope if you are reading this, don't be discouraged by the other comments. Show up, engage, and you will get a good grade, period

Dr. Chesney, though very informative and wise in terms of his knowledge of urbanism, is one of the most disorganized and confusing professors I have had at Vanderbilt. If you are not planning to major in urbanism or anything around this subject, please beware of taking this class. Altogether, I wish Dr. Chesney could change his grading system.
Man is talm bout how his life goal is to become a professor at Harvard LIKE DAWG U NOT EVEN GOOD ENOUGH FOR COMMUNITY COLLEGE GANG; and NO ONE CARES how u been to skid row and allat.

REALLY goofy guy. Says some outlandish stuff and the lectures are just whatever he wants to yap about. Interesting grading where he gives top half As on tests and bottom half Bs but with presentations and extra credit you will probably finish with an A or A-. Strongly opinionated is an understatement but he's such a character its entertaining.

The course topic is genuinely interesting and had a lot of potential, but the class itself was very disappointing. The lectures often felt slow, repetitive, and unengaging, and the material could have been taught much more efficiently. Overall, the course drained my energy more than it helped me learn. I would not recommend it.

This is the worst class I've ever taken at Vanderbilt. Professor Ajogbeje doesn't like to teach things that are important to homework or exams. He talks about his background and Nigerian heritage for half of the class, and the other half is about warning us that he could fail everyone if we don't pay attention. His exam is extremely difficult.

He is very controlling and trying to be dominant. Please be aware when considering taking his classes. He thinks he is the King of the classroom and is very stubborn (even when he had nothing to brag about).
bad class but highly recommend this professor's SO, that's interesting, do you understand?? please pay attention. BUT, SO, BUT, SO, welcome to class.

Ajogbeje is bad. Students don't learn much from his classes. It's a waste of time. His lecture is dry and boring. All he does is pointing his long fingers at students and yelling them to pay attention to the low quality lecture he's delivering.
Prof. Ajogbeje is a great professor overall. He teaches a very fair curriculum and is easy to follow along with.
With all due respect, he is a diversity/DEI hire in the MHS department. He is NOT qualified.

His class is SO boring. He keeps yelling at students to pay attention, but the teaching itself makes it hard to stay engaged. He repeats the same content constantly and doesn't seem qualified to teach applied AI or Python. Any advanced question seems to throw him off, so the course feels frustrating. I'm confused why Vanderbilt hired him for this.

absolutely no constructive feedback allowed. he thinks everything he does is perfect and good for the students. he doesn't listen to his students or adjusts his style of teaching. yes, he is passionate but NOT effective.
Korede Ajogbeje is a good professor. I highly recommend his classes though he doesn't allow any criticism!
so-so class, so-so professor. Highly recommend, do you understand? SO
i took his AI class. it was pretty average. the class itself wasn't difficult but the professor was very difficult to deal with.

Professor Korede is an amazing professor. He is very lenient in grading while still giving you solid feedback on what you can improve. I received an A to A- on most assignments. As long as you show interest in the class and put in effort on the assignments, he is very forgiving. I once turned in an assignment late and received no penalty.
difficult to work with and passive aggressive from a lot of my interactions with her. definitely did not have the same experience as everyone else.
Shes a good CORE prof. She truly wants to learn from students.

Pro:
-code in R helped visualize graphs (lotka-volterra model)
-no attendance
-records all lectures
-you can redo weekly R assignments
-she does test corrections

Con:
-lecture heavy, sometimes boring
-Exam questions were unnecessarily tricky sometimes (e.g., asking about the pattern of island biogeography among correct mechanisms)

Malu is a kind person and her class is relatively easy, but her lectures are difficult to keep up with because they are only pictures and graphs. The best part about this class is that the exams are worth so little, so if you do well on the homeworks and problem sets you will have some room to not do great on the exams. Take if you are interested.

I really liked how she used scientific graphs/papers as examples of concepts we learned in the beginning of the semester and connected them to new material we were learning throughout the year. She listens to her students and asks for our opinions. I really liked the class and think she is a great person

Malu is such a great person and respected professor, but not the best lecturer. I would often get bored during lectures and not pay attention. You also never knew what to expect for exams. Some were super easy and others were very hard. There are test corrections and lots of assignments for grades.

Malu is a fantastic prof. Her lectures are not dense, just very thorough. She also guides you through R studio, but makes it VERY manageable for beginners. She listens to class feedback and adjusts the course accordingly. The course structure is pretty complicated but that just makes it less boring :) There are midterms, reading assignments, R, etc
Horribly boring class but pretty easy assignments, tests are okay (the first was badly made but she took feedback and improved the next ones)

One of the more fun professors I've had here! When she doesn't finish a lecture (which happens often), she just puts it off to the next class. She's very chill and cut content from exams if needed. Her exams are free response on a Word document submitted on Brightspace. The hardest part of the class was the R coding assignments
Malu is super interested in the subject and her class topics are extremely interesting.

Malu is a wonderful professor and an amazing person (definitely a rare combo at Vandy). She is always available and guides you through what she expects in her assignments. She is truly passionate about conservation so get ready to reciprocate the vigor. You will get a decent grade and learn so much if you have a good attitude. Taking Ecology too.
Malu was great and may some complex concepts super simple with her in class examples!
