her powerpoints are really hard to decipher, because she mostly just puts images and graphs on them. you have to do the discussions, but she grades them kind of weirdly, so you have no idea how much you actually participate. tests are graded really randomly, but at least she gives lots of extra credit, up to nine points a test. pretty nice person.

Her powerpoints are not that useful, and she's not really clear either. There are Excel exercises every week, and a lot of equations, but it's not terrible. Her tests aren't very easy, but better than average for the BSCI department. Still, she's a really nice person and I strongly recommend her to other students. Read the book, though.

Professor Lewis is the best professor I have had at Vanderbilt. He cares about his students and wants us to succeed. He is always open during office hours, engaging in lectures, and there is no homework. Your grade is made up of 3 tests and a project, but he gives you a clear study guide of exactly what is on the test.

Professor Lewis is great! Most of the learning will come from writing memos and case studies, but the lectures are helpful in preparing you for your major assignments. Grading is a bit harsh, but if you take advantage of office hours, you should do fine. There are also plenty of extra credit opportunities.
Good lectures and very clear study guides for tests. Assigns lots of readings, but honestly it is worth your time to focus on in-class material and the study guides more.

You will learn in this class -- Lewis is a fantastic teacher and really cares. He will know everyone's name and face in a week. He applies the class to the real world and does case studies. That said, I wrote 9 pages for tests and got 3 words of feedback and averaged a B. This class is just not worth the grading scale.

Prof. Lewis is the best lecturer! I learned so much from his class. He always makes new presentation slides each class (never uses old ones). I truly admire his compassion, understanding, and respect. His class is very engaging and people never get bored.

Take at least one of his classes while you're at Vandy. It's a life-changing experience!

Professor Lewis is great, very kind and knowledgeable. His TAs grade everything and are quite harsh and provide little to no feedback. I learned a lot in this class but be prepared to read a lot. Two midterms, an essay, and a final.

Lewis was a strong and engaging professor who I learned a lot from, but the class was poorly organized in my opinion. Felt rushed at the end with writing and is very hard to get an A. Heavy readings but can usually get away with skimming, and pretty much all grading is writing. Interesting class but not an easy A by any means.

The most caring professor—his lectures are fascinating, and you'll learn so much! However, he's the toughest grader ever. Once you figure out what he looks for, you can earn high scores, but it still takes serious prep and effort. Totally worth it if you want to be challenged and grow!

His lecture is the best I have ever experienced! He is so knowledgeable and accessible outside class. Although you have to read a lot, it's worth it. You will learn so much from this professor. His grading is fair; if you work hard, grade won't disappoint you. He's a very humble person despite all the reputations, titles, and papers he published.

Really kind and smart guy but it is true that he is a really tough grader. I wish he would've given more feedback on how to get more points. Definitely not an easy class, but came out of it realizing I actually learned a lot, and he is funny. Lectures are boring sometimes. Got by with dumping my lecture and review session notes into the exams.

Thought professors were supposed to lift students up and support them??  Not this professor!  If you are a hard working student RUN!!!  you will never be graded fairly.  All your hard work will get you a B- if lucky!  The most arrogant and uncaring professor I have ever had!!!!

If you take this professor you will
Watch all your hard work and your grade washed down the drain!!!  DO NOT take this professor he doesnt care about his students at all even if you work hard !!  Unfair grader,  see your gpa plummet.  Impossible to do well!  And not a nice guy.

Professor Lewis is one of the best professors and people I have ever met. He is well-respected by everyone that takes his class. He gives great, informative lectures and students are well-prepared to do well in the class. He is a tougher grader than most, but it is worth it to feel like you actually earned the work and learned in the class. 10/10

Professor Lewis is amazing and genuinely cares about each of his students. This class is pretty easy as long as you attend class and thoroughly complete the review sheets, which he will help you with in office hours. Two midterms, a paper, and a final. Highly recommend any class with Professor Lewis!

Prof. Lewis is a fantastic lecturer and super passionate about students' learning. Exams are entirely free response and his essay grading can be tedious, but prepare thoroughly and you will be fine. Great introduction to American politics for aspiring PSCI/PPS majors. Memorized everyone's names by the first day of class!
Informative course, I wrote some poems I'm really proud of. Detailed feedback from the professor.
The sweetest professor I've ever had, she made our class engaging and informative! She gives amazing feedback for your poems and is so easy to talk to. 10/10 would recommend taking her class!!!!!!

Very passionate about teaching. Always ready to get our class going and goes the extra mile to help us become better writers and better people. Would 100% take any of her classes if I ever got the opportunity to again.
Such a fun and stress-free semester learning about and writing poetry. I walked out of our last class feeling like I was in Dead Poet's Society.
She is the best professor ever, cares so much about her students, super relatable, makes writing poetry fun (coming from a stem student), super easy class and best HCA credit out there

Professor Hutton is an excellent instructor! I would highly recommend his Maymester 2120 course. It was fully online with 4 assignments. He used SAS which I was not familiar with but he provided a lot of help. He was available for questions and explained the concepts really well. The course was fast-paced but Professor Hutton made it manageable.

Super chill and pleasant guy! Class was very easy; HW twice a month and 3 online exams, no final. He curves exams that people do poorly on. I did HW during the optional discussion so TAs could check it and I could get it out of the way. I didn't really learn anything, but it is an easy addition to your schedule if youre taking other hard classes.

chillest man you will ever meet. all online, take home, open note exams. content wasn't too hard. he has detailed slides, so during exams I literally copied the exact steps on his slideshows. homework assignment once a week. super straightforward class. would recommend over any other professor.
Excellent class! Dr. Hutton made learning statistics a lot easier than my high school teacher. He is friendly and cares about his students.
Very very easy class. Tests are online. Just make sure you go to class (TopHat class), and you'll get an easy A. Weekly assignments take like 30-45 mins.

Dr. Hutton is clear, organized, and genuinely cares about his students. Lectures are straightforward. The exams are open note and fair. The pace can feel fast at times but if you keep up it's very manageable.
Dr. Hutton explains things very well and makes a difficult subject easy to learn. He's an excellent instructor!

Hutton is the absolute GOAT. Literally thanks you for coming to class every day. Every exam is online, open note, and you have plenty of time. Very straightforward and clear slides that give you everything you need. Weekly assignments take about 30 minutes and are graded lightly. He seems like the best choice for Peabody Stats.

Dr. Hutton is one of the best professors to take for Peabody stats. He's clear in his explanations and the tests are open book. I usually struggle with math but this class has been much better than previous math classes I've taken.

Despite the amount of asynch videos and slides he provides on a weekly basis, the pace is way too fast and very accelerated (especially if you have never taken stats before).  It was very hard to follow, process and understand.
Awful. Would rather have a root canal.
4 tests. 8 assignments. Class is fine.

The TopHat notes are what you need for the exams! The homework doesn't exactly reflect what is on the test, and I think for me I have a harder time with online tests in general. I have a weaker math background than most and found this class to be harder than expected.

Absolutely loved Dr. Hutton! You can work with classmates on all assignments and the tests were all open-note take-home tests. As someone who really struggles in math, this is the math class to take. I learned a lot but it was not super difficult.

Attendance is not mandatory but there are tophat questions during class that make up your participation grade. Tests were take home on Brightspace, and they are reasonable if you review the lecture slides. Seven weekly homework assignments were fairly easy as well. Good intro course to statistics, I recommend taking Dr. Hutton.
Great lecturer and goes through so many examples that makes the homework doable.

Great guy but not a great professor. Lectures are hard to follow and he isn't accessible at all outside of class. Disappeared for 2 weeks before the final. Test grading scheme is incredibly weird, where a tiny mistake means you lose 7% due to his "3-point system". Encourages you to argue with him, but shuts you down if you do. Needs to retire lol.

Homework after every class and due on the same day. Tests are doable if you grind decently beforehand and understand your materials. Sometimes he just disappears but overall if you try to talk to him he's willing to connect you to stuff. Good luck!
Great professor. Very didactic. The material can be complicated, but is easily distilled, and exams were very very fair.

Great guy, good lectures, easy tests. The class was structed as 50 minutes of lecture, and then 25 minutes to do the homework due by the next day, which was usually easy to complete in that time. The class is not very hard (easier than Circuits 1), and if you want an A you can easily get it. He posts extra practice problems if you really need it.

He is a very nice professor, compared to the other EE faculty i met. The class is not hard, but his exams are somewhat random since there's no practice exams at all. Also the homework is hard and not that relevant to his exams. Overall, not too hard to get A, but you have to teach yourself to do it.

While he has a good attitude, his exams are very unreasonable. He expects you to do 2 hours of work on a 50 min exam. Studying more won't help because his exams tend to focus entirely on 1 random topic out of all the material he goes over. His lectures are over complicated and monotonous, so you are better off teaching yourself.
Easy class. Know mathematica like the back of your hand and you'll be fine. Tests are way easier than homework.
Easy tests, problem sets are a little too hard, nice guy, helpful with homework. Final is a little harder than the midterms

This class is much easier than 112, and Reed is just the man. Test questions are from homework. The night before homework is due he sits on his email ready to help. He wants everyone to do well and works hard to make it that way. If you dont like mathematica dont take the class.
Great teacher, explains everything well, tests are reasonable, nice guy. Nuff said

It was the first time Professor Reed taught this course, but it was hard to tell. Although a lot of the class was deriving the formulas we would use, he made it a lot less painful than usual. His lectures were very clear and his grading standards were very fair. The subject was difficult, but I still learned a lot and enjoyed the experience.

Great professor. Both him and Weller teach this class with different sections, and I'd recommend Reed. This class is a million times easier than 112 for lack of hand-solving problems - but you still end up learning quite a lot. Reed knows Mathematica well, and is a great communicator.
Class management felt chaotic at times. Course content was enjoyable, though not uniquely so compared to similar courses. Was her first year teaching and it shows.

Warm and helpful with all students. Took her in her first year teaching, so she was a bit inexperienced at lecturing, but she clearly knows her stuff. Class readings were short and enjoyable. Much of the class comes down to reviewing others' work, which can be repetitive, but is easy.

Very smart and easy to talk to, especially at office hours. Her research isn't really aligned with the course material, so her teaching can be hard to follow at times. No slides and no posted lectures. Pop quizzes are announced ahead of time, pretty easy, and give a lot of extra credit.

The content of his class was interesting, but every single class is just 50 minutes of lecturing. The lectures are all 40-60 slides long and he talks sooo fast. Theres also 150-450 pages of reading a week which is insane. Worst part is he grades you subjectively, with no clear criteria. Exams cover niche characters or happenings in readings

The nicest most passionate professor EVER. He loves what he does and I find it so inspiring. Took him for Roman law of delicts- I was terrible at it. This class was not my strong suit by any means and I lowkey thought class was extremely boring . But I love this man and would defend him till the death

This is a class where you really have to enjoy the topic to like the professor. He is clearly very passionate about his subject, but the readings are long and can be quite dull. He also lectures basically every class, which might not be everyone's cup of tea. The class is really easy however, and the workload isn't bad if you just skim the readings

This was a difficult class, and Dr. McGinn is an exacting professor. He is also extremely knowledgeable, helpful, and caring. 3150 is great for students who are willing to think hard about the cases, attend lectures, ask questions, and integrate feedback. Highly recommend if you fit this description and want to improve your legal reasoning/writing
Incredibly harsh grader.

Don't take this class if not interested in classics at all. You will be so uninterested and unmotivated to learn anything (seriously, I cannot recall a single thing I've learned this year). He's a tough grader - if you normally get As on papers, expect Bs from him. His lectures are paragraphs in a ppt. Nonetheless, he is caring and accommodating.

Professor mcginn is great, he seems to genuinely care about the material which makes an 8am lecture entertaining. He tends to assign a lot of reading but you can get by doing a lot less. Test are only hard because most people dont study until the night before, if you pay attention during lectures and study earlier youll be fine

Prof. McGinn is very respected in his field, and it's easy to tell that he cares a lot about the material he teaches. Still, his lectures are fairly boring for non-Classics majors, and there are only two graded assignments all semesters. He's not a super tough grader, but he does take attendance. Definitely not the most engaging class I've taken.

Professor McGinn is obviously knowledgeable about Latin/ancient Rome, but this class was very dry. We mostly translated for homework outside of class and then went over it in class. There was only a midterm and final, so tests were very important for the grade. Work hard and you'll get an A but it's a lot of work.

He's a really nice guy and is very smart. Few assignments, 1 short paper, 1 long paper, and a midterm and a final. The class was boring but it was definitely a class that requires little work in and out of the class. Professor is really great, but only take if you need the credit or need a class that requires no work (but ok w not getting an A)

I highly recommend Professor McGinn and his Roman Law class, the best taste of law school one can get as an undergrad. All based on the class's case law text book, the papers can be a challenge, but Prof McGinn, extremely knowledgeable in his field, is a valuable resource to improving your writing and refining your analysis.

Dr. McGinn is incredibly understanding and is always there for you. The class involves a lot of reading, but the classes are generally just lectures, so it's not always really necessary. Beware: the only grades are two exams and a midterm paper. He's very lenient about grading essays, but vocab/identification sections of tests are difficult.
Hilarious professor, gives a lot of reading but he's by far the most entertaining professor I've had.

Dr. McGinn is a really fun teacher to have. Class is a standard advanced Latin course, taking turns translating material that was assigned. He's very understanding when it comes to issues outside of class interfering with performance or due dates. Tests are just translating a couple passages that were covered. Would recommend if you enjoy Latin.

Really energetic, and obviously enjoys the subject. Participation is a grade, but class consists entirely of translating Latin assigned at the end of the previous class. Might not be a good class for those with anxiety. Professor is more than willing to help with students and be accommodating when they're having difficulties. Typical Latin grading.
Whoever said weird vibes in the class nailed it. She's great at what she does and I really the class at first. After a while it felt like the class revolved more around her than the learning

Dr. Klyce is a sweet person. The structure is good—graded on many things. However, she is not lenient. You must be in person to turn in homework, or it's late. You're allowed to work together, but if your homework is too similar to others, you can get flagged for cheating. Overall unclear instructions and high maintenance for a 1000 level class.
weird vibe in the class

Great Professor! I did not take the lab, just the lecture. There are some quizzes, but as long as you do the problem sets, your grade should be fine! One of the best professors! Would highly recommend this professor if you need to take a science class as a requirement. She also gives a lot of extra credit opportunities!
Dr Klyce is amazing and if you have basically any prior knowledge of geology you will get an A in this class. It's called rocks for jocks for a reason.

Dr Klyce is absolutely amazing, and EES 1510 lecture is super easy. If you have basically any prior knowledge of geology, you will receive an A in the class. She's super helpful and I was extremely comfortable asking questions. The only thing is that there is a lot of reading but you don't have much homework outside of it, so it's a good trade off.

Class was very messy (changing field trip dates, changing attendance policy mid-semester). Labs were ridiculously long (took most of the class more than 14 hours on multiple labs). With that said, Dr. Klyce is a good professor (if you're looking to take 1510, ignore this. it's very easy and fun, which tricked me into taking this diabolical class)
The class is way too difficult and odee for a 100 level course.

I love Dr. Klyce, she's a fantastic teacher who is passionate about teaching Geology. But the amount of work for the 1 credit lab, and difficult grading of the problem sets have hurt my experience, too much work for a 1000-level science course, I will not be taking another EES class in the future.

The reason why recent reviews are so much worse is because she is actively trying to change the geology curriculum to reflect the difficulty of physics and chem, rather than oceanography or astronomy.

Not worth the lab credit. Dr. Klyce is on some ego trip and unless you're a carbon copy of her do not take this class. She's the LEAST understanding teacher I have ever had. Makes tests impossibly hard unless your an EES major. I actually really enjoy geology but bc of her I'm not adding a double major.

The lab was significantly harder than the actual class. The lab midterm was ridiculous but only counts for 15% of the grade. If you are looking to just fulfill the lab requirement, DON'T TAKE. It's not worth it unless you're really into rocks.
Bad bad bad - do not take if you care about your gpa and time!

From the class itself, I am traumatized, but deep down, but I do think Dr. Klyce does care for her students. I wish the grading rubric were clearer for lab reports and that the trips were more planned out in advance. The class is stressful, but Dr. Klyce does excel at explaining difficult content. Challenging class!
She makes it obvious when she has a bad day.
Hard to understand lecture style.TA did a lot of the classes.  Exams were difficult. Tons of homework.

Dr. Ravi's lectures were difficult to follow. He missed multiple weeks of class due to work trips (the TAs lead the lecture, but they were difficult to understand). Dr. Ravi gave no flexibility on grading or assignments despite missing class. The exams were very difficult and the homework was long (and due at 10pm which was irritating)
This is for 2205. Class is very difficult conceptually. Cares about students but exams are difficult and lectures hard to follow.
Great professor but terrible exams. Class averages were in the low 70s all semester, no curve and only one small extra credit assignment. TA and him grade out of vengeance

Had him for Statics and MOM and he cares the most about students actually learning by far. He cares about every student and is equally passionate about the material itself and the students understanding/wellbeing. Most clear teaching style of any professor I've had.

Super nice guy that genuinely wants you to learn. Takes the time to answer questions and make sure students understand with example problems. Homework does take a while but prepares you well for the tests which are pretty reasonable considering the content

Dr. Ravi is a very kind professor who really cares about our learning. He teaches in a variety of styles to work with different learning types. If you do the homework and pay attention in class, the tests are very reasonable.

Ravi is the best professor I've had at Vanderbilt. He genuinely cares about each student and will do so much to help you succeed. He makes a lot of dorky jokes in class and maintains a very low-stress, laid-back, friendly atmosphere. He says he wants to be your statics "coach", not your teacher, and I think he embodies that really well.

Best professor I've had. I normally end up teaching myself the material in class, but I actually feel engaged when I am in Dr. Ravi's class. If you show up to class and pay attention you'll get a good grade. He's also the boy.

Great choice for Statics. He really really cares for his students and will help you as much as he can if you reach out to him. Lectures are important to attend. Tests are actually very fair and are similar to questions from homework/class problems/practice exams. Funny in class and teaches well. Put in effort and you will do well.

Dr. Ravi is a great choice for statics: he clearly cares about the student's learning and well-being, and his jokes and demonstrations made (in-person) lecture entertaining. As long as you put effort into this class all semester, you should be good.

Dry material, lectures don't make up for it. Duddu goes from very clear to bumbling later in the semester. Learning from the (lots of) homework helpful and a must. Tests fair but a time crunch and nitpicked for deductions. Two projects that feel pointless and unnecessarily difficult. Overall a slog, but Duddu genuinely cares and has some good jokes

I had him for statics and his classes were always enjoyable. Despite having him early in the morning he was engaging enough to keep me paying attention and he does a great job breaking down concepts and presenting them in an understandable manor.

Prof. Duddus lectures are solid. He has a pretty good mix of theory and solving problems. Sometimes he can get confused when someone asks him a question, but otherwise is very good at communicating his expectations and what you need to know. He has several group projects. His tests arent hard, but time can be an issue. Overall great guy.

Your entire grade is determined by 5 pop quizzes. If you fail these, you will see the consequences. There is no system by which this professor seems to grade. You are better off taking AP lit. I regret taking this class, I've learned little more than I can learn in a day.
interesting
Would not do again
She is unclear in her grading, has no rubric or doesnt show it to students, and assigns pointless work. She emphasizes handwriting over depth in writing, and treats us like elementary age children.
She is very hard
Don't take her
Most of her "teaching" is just in-class discussion. Very little lecturing happens, and her subjective grading criteria are unclear. Very frustrating class

Whilst I initially enjoyed this class, it soon became the worst mistake ever. She is absolutely arrogant and honestly a nightmare. Her attitude towards students does not create a good learning environment in the slightest. Save your time, energy and dignity and stay far far away from taking anything she offers.

This professor is amazing. I got switched into this class because of the req but the course material was easy to follow. Participation also matters so make sure you do that. She is a great person, as well! Enjoyed her class & it's easy if you do as you're told.
If Reddit was a philosophy professor

Great dude, and a very engaging class, only undermined by a skewed, paternalistic approach. I took this class for giggles as a senior and was a bit appalled at how confidently wrong or misleading Ismail was on many topics. Not a fault of Ismail, but a fault of an approach of “I will teach you every topic in my own words.” Inevitably very biased.

I absolutely loved Prof. Kurun. Easily one of the best professors I've had so far at Vandy. He truly cares about his students' learning and is very passionate about what he does. Tests and cumulative paper were fair and very doable. Utilize his office hours and go to class!

Professor Kurun is one of the most remarkable instructors in the philosophy department. He truly teaches you how to comprehend, argue, and write PROPERLY. Not many people in your life will pay such close attention to these vital skills. Moreover, Kurun is engaging, funny, and inspiring all while the grading is easy and he loves hearing feedback.

Ismail is excellent at explaining complex philosophy readings in a way anyone can understand and appreciate. I highly recommend you take him if you plan on taking a philosophy class. With reasonable effort, anyone can do well in his classes. He is also very available and helpful for office hours.

I took Intro to ethics with Ismail and he was great. The best part about him is that he will change parts of the class based off feedback throughout the semester. His lectures were interesting and he then encouraged us to use what we learned during discussions. Some topics were sensitive but he navigated them well. I recommend taking his class!

For some reason, wouldn't let me tag the course code as PHIL 1005, but this is the class I took with Kurun. He genuinely cares about his students and their success in his class. He asks for feedback and adjusts his class based on what his students need. Assigns readings per class that aren't too difficult. Discussion based class. 100% recommend!

Ismail is an outstanding professor and one of the best teachers I had at Vanderbilt. I took his class to fulfill a degree requirement, with no prior interest in philosophy. However, his lectures and passion for the subject made me deeply enjoy and appreciate it. He genuinely enjoys teaching and is great at it. I would love to take his class again.

Overall great class! Ismail really knows his philosophy and communicates concepts very clearly. His lectures were extremely informative, and he was always willing to meet with students outside of class, which was really helpful. Also, Ismail really cares about his students. HIGHLY recommend taking a class with him!!

Paige is super caring, funny, relatable and passionate. She is extremely encouraging, gives amazing feedback, and facilitates great discussions. I really enjoyed the readings and the creativity Paige brought to the class. Assignments were somewhat challenging, but interesting and helpful for writing skills. Take your W class with her!!!

Professor Russell is a fantastic professor who cares deeply about her work, her students, and the legal field. I am typically a straight A student, but had to work hard to achieve an A- in her class. Despite this, I would ABSOLUTELY take her again. I learned a lot from her knowledge and enthusiasm for the law, both in class and during office hours.

Professor Russell is a fantastic lecturer who cares deeply about her work, her students, and the legal field. I worked very hard to achieve an A-, despite typically being a straight A student. Despite this, I would ABSOLUTELY take her class again because I learned so much. Go to office hours!! She is knowledgeable and wants her students to succeed.

Prof Russell was great, she was funny, and our class had great chemistry, which led to zero cold calling and good participation grades all around. The toughest part about her class is that a 95 is an A, but it is definitely possible. I highly recommend taking her class. I've never learned more about the law!

She was funny and charismatic and there's lots of extra credit opportunities to help boost your grade to that tough A (95) baseline so it's definitely doable. Didn't cold call but I had a really active section so if people are participating she won't just randomly call on someone else. Midterm was easy, mostly short response, final is fun.

Professor Russell has been my absolute favorite professor at Vanderbilt so far. Her passion about the study of law and genuine interest in her students success is very clear. This class is a must if you are thinking about going into law school.

Professor Russell is the best. There are a couple low rated reviews for her from Covid years and I urge everyone to disregard them. She is so knowledgeable, very helpful, and super entertaining. She will work with you and go out of her way to make sure you are doing the best you can be. Also ties in current events when applicable which is awesome.

Professor Russell is awesome. You can tell she is genuinely passionate about what she teaches and really wants all of her students to succeed, beyond the classroom especially. PSCI-3260 is great for anyone thinking about law school and I highly recommend it.

Most difficult class I've taken at Vandy—but worth it. Dr. Russell is so intelligent and this class covered many legal topics, including current events. Readings were long but manageable and she covered most in class anyways. The essay topics and moot court were interesting and very insightful. Recommend doing every extra credit opportunity.

Dr. Russell's class helped me realize that I do enjoy learning about the law. She definitely cares about the issues at hand. Lectures were dry, and the feedback she gave on the 2 hypos and the moot court opinion felt a bit arbitrary. Worst thing is that she wants a 95 for an A - in my opinion, that's ridiculous and enough to steer clear from her.
Hard to get an A (95+). Great professor but seems a little distant with freshmen who take her class. Really funny and usually engaging lectures.

Teaches out of the textbook for the most part. Readings can range from 20 to 100+ pages, so be ready. Doesn't cold call. Lectures can get a little boring but generally not too bad. Two hypotheticals, a decently hefty midterm, and moot court which r were pretty interesting. Generally a very nice, accomidating professor---fs take her if ur pre-law.

Interesting and Thought-Provoking lectures, but the class was hard to excel in. Never skip and try to talk in class but do not need to do every reading. TA grades and it can seem pretty harsh, but she graded the final lightest of all which helped a bunch. Would take another class with her because the content really makes you think, but consider GPA

Despite the volume of material in this class, Professor Russell makes each lesson digestible and easy to follow. This class is quite fun if you're willing to speak up and really engage with the content. The hypotheticals and final project are intellectually stimulating and what you learn will really stay with you even after the class ends.

Professor Russell's lectures are incredibly impactful. Her assignments are always relevant and grading criteria is made clear. She is very open to feedback and cares about the success of each student. Outside of class, she is always willing to give advice and guidance on pre-law/law school related matters. I highly recommend taking her classes!

You can tell she cares deeply about her students and the Constitution.  The assignments are fair and she provides a rubric before-hand as well as old exemplars from previous classes.  Strongly recommend.
My GOAT Alexander Jacobs. Great class to take for AXLE credit, study hard, take good notes and you will do well.

Conspiracy theories was probably my favourite class in undergrad. The lectures are very engaging, fun, and informative. Take good notes and make good use of your notecard on exams. The exams were all very fair and you can revise the 2 midterms to be one score category higher. The workload wasn't high but don't procrastinate on the poster.

Prof. Jacobs is great, but I feel there is too much work for a core class. We have 2 midterms and a non-cumulative final on terms in the course (not too difficult) and an assigned book + poster project. He's a fascinating lecturer and incredibly smart, but I just think there is a bit too much work.

Dr Jacobs is truly a gem. Vandy is lucky to have someone who cares as much about his students as Dr Jacobs does. Class is pretty easy, especially if you do the readings he assigns beforehand. My advice: Focus more on soaking up knowledge from Dr Jacobs than on a grade; he's brilliant, and you'll get more out of the class if you engage yourself.

Lots of readings for homework, but there were only 3 MC reading quizzes, 3 writing assignments and a final. Class is lecture only, but he explains concepts very well. he is a very nice professor and such a sweetheart!

Absolutely loved this class. Prof Jacobs is genuinely such a smart guy who offers impressive insights into the content you cover in class and makes the work very manageable. Sets up his grading rubric in a unique way that you will appreciate once you're in it. The amount of reading seems extreme at first, but the discussions make it easy to digest.

Professor Jacobs is amazing. Fascinating class. He does not lecture at you, and the class is very engaging. If you do the readings and participate in class, you will succeed on the exams. He doesn't try to trick you. Very creative and fun assignments. Very fair grader. He cares less about perfection and more about understanding main themes.

Professor AJ might possibly be the best professor at Vanderbilt. He is so generous with grading, prioritizing genuine learning over stress of grades. In class, he is always as hilarious and charming as he is knowledgeable. I suggest that everyone take a class with him if given the chance. Plus, some really fun yet unique assignments.

Religion Politics Culture with Professor Jacobs made me switch my major so that I could take more classes like Professor Jacobs'. He is one of the smartest and most engaging people I have ever met. Take any class you can with him!

Dr. Jacobs is a prof I will remember as one of the best educators I've had in my life. Extremely passionate, energetic, very eloquent. A type of person to talk to for hours and never get bored. Easy grading, nothing tricky. Gives you a token you can redeem if you get a bad grade. Makes it easy to become passionate with what he teaches. Amazing.

Amazing professor. Probably one of the smartest two or three people I've ever met. Unbelievably well read and has forgotten more about history than I will likely ever know. Great guy and just loved his class.

Professor Jacobs has the best-managed class at Vanderbilt. Your grade in AC consists of 5 quizzes, one midterm, a term paper, and a final. But, if you mess up on a quiz, he gives you a token to take everything but the final again. If you need AXLE American credit, TAKE THIS CLASS!

Professor Jacobs is one of the best professors I've had here. He really knows his stuff, and does his best to reduce the stress of graded assignments and exams. Participation in class was extra credit, so you could get full attendance points even if you don't like talking in classes. Highly recommend.

Jacobs is super smart and down to earth. He goes above and beyond to make the content approachable and enjoyable. He really wants you to focus on critical thinking and appreciation of the material rather than worrying about your grade. Has a points based system that lets you focus on growth & improving your writing instead of stressing over grades.

Professor Jacobs is amazing! He cares about his students and you will learn so much from him if you show up to class! He isn't a difficult grader (if you do decent work you'll get an A) and will go out of his way to make sure you succeed!

Professor Kim's course was not hard if you put in the work, his instructions were sometimes unclear, but he does want his students to do well. Much of the content in this class involve presentations and group work.
Hes literally the nicest guy in the world. Can tell he genuinely cares so much about students! He is a little hard to understand, but if you show you put in effort, you will get an a.

Professor Kim is very nice but not the clearest teacher. He is hard to understand and goes on tangents frequently. There is a lot of outside of class reading and he wants you to participate a lot. Even though there is a lot of work, none of it is that difficult, it is more just busy work.

Dr Kim is very intelligent and loves what he does. He teaches content that he created himself, which is amazing. He also loves his students so much that he goes out of his way to ask how each person is. However, the class lectures are so disorganized that it is nearly impossible to tell what is going on. There is a lot of homework.

Professor Kim an incredibly caring and intelligent teacher. He fosters and environment where you can speak freely and often. If you are someone who enjoys thinking critically his readings are riveting. There are lots of opportunities for extra credit and as long as you show genuine effort you will get an A in this class! Would take again!

Prof Kim is a very passionate teacher but if you are looking to take an easy W class you probably shouldn't take his class. There is a lot of work in terms of presentations but it's not super writing heavy. Unfortunately, instructions are unclear most of the time and you'll have to rely on your class president who you elect to explain assignments.

Very unorganized lecture. You will read at least 4-5 books that are >100 pages long or read equally long poems. He will shift assignment deadlines randomly (in a bad way) and give group presentation projects almost every week. Syllabus for this class means nothing. Please don't take his class. Don't trust the ratings here. NOT WORTH IT.

Dr. Kim is an incredible professor who has not only an incredible knowledge of the source materials but is also very caring about his students. However, this is a very heavy course load, sometimes many assignments each week. I recommend this class, but during a lighter schedule. As long as you put in work, you will get the grade you deserve.

Great person and very knowledgeable but a ridiculous amount of assignments for a 1000 level W class. Posts confusing rubrics and difficult readings and posts assignments late the night before class expecting them to be done the next day.
Fun class, not hard to get an A if you do the reading and work

Dr,Kim really does care and keeps an open mind to everyone's perspectives. However, grading criteria was often unclear and he would notify us of HW assignments with pretty short turnaround. The learning curve for how he talks and comes up with ideas is insane-- first 3-6 weeks you will really struggle to understand him. It gets a little better.

I LOVE Professor Jeong-Oh Kim! He is so sweet, so smart, and truly takes an interest in his students having a good time learning the course material. I don't normally go to office hours but I typically met up with him outside of class a few times because a) he always gave constructive feedback, and b) I just wanted to spend time with him.

During the first few weeks, I had no idea what I was doing cuz the material was hard to understand. There was also a lot of homework at first, but afterwards, the class became a lot of fun with outside classes and group projects. Dr. Kim's a really lenient grader, and he offers so much extra credit too. He's also pretty funny

It's very evident that Professor Kim cares about his students very much, and I wouldn't be surprised if everyone in the class got an A. The class also really felt like a community. That being said, the amount of work assigned was way too much for this course, readings were sometimes quite difficult, and the expectations were often unclear.

Professor Kim is very caring and passionate about poetry/literature. His lectures are difficult to understand but with time you begin to understand his nuances. 2-3 writing assignments a week based on readings. If you don't like reading, this class is NOT for you, during the semester we read 5-6 books in their entirety.

Professor Robinson is fantastic, and I learned so much from her. Her lectures are super interesting. She is a fair grader and makes her criteria for an A clear and achievable. I am typically an A student, but my A- was well deserved (if not generous). Take her class!! She is among the very best professors at Vanderbilt.

Robinson will expect you to learn an incredible amount about architecture, and doing so will be very timely. So, steer away if you just want an elective credit. However, if you are interested in the topic, she is a romantic about the buildings she teaches, and you will also fall in love with architecture!
If you're a HART major, then get prepared for the exams. If you're not, then run!

One of the best classes I took at Vanderbilt. Robinson could be intense in her excitement for the material but was animated and engaging and brought Ancient Rome to life in class. Not the easiest, but I learned so much. Definitely go to her office hours. Robinson rocks!

This class is easy A if you do the work and readings and show up. Unfortunately I was major league struggling this year and tbh the B- was way too merciful I'm still shocked I didn't fail lol. She is so compassionate I honestly have no idea why she put up with me, and the content is super interesting and worth studying fully, not just for a grade.

Dr. Robinson is awesome. Her classes are nearly entirely lectures, but they are really engaging. This class is a ton of work with readings, reading responses, and papers, but she doesn't grade too harshly. This is very much a get out what you put in class, and with Dr. Robinson, it's a lot of fun.

Prof. Robinson is clearly very knowledge and passionate about the subject she teaches. Unfortunately this can lead to confusion about exact material and disorganized study guides as she sometimes forgets her students are not experts in the subject like she is.

Dr. Robinson is awesome. She's clearly really passionate about the subject and goes over a ton of content in class. Theres a midterm a book review a project and final so not a ton of grades but enough different ones where it's not too bad
This was a great class! Robinson's a riot and kept class entertaining. Challenging class, exams were heavy but fair as long as we prepared well--find a study group!

This is the worst class ever. she thinks its the only class we are all enrolled in i guess because she requires WAY too much of us. worst, most chaotic lessons. feel like i didnt learn anything. and the tests are extremely anxiety inducing and a total free for all

Prof Robinson is a good lecturer and the class is fairly interesting; however, the class was very disorganized and felt inconsistent as we would rarely finish material meant for class even after consistently lecturing 3-5 mins over. Despite these inconveniences, Robinson cares about the material, and succeeds in bringing the past to life.

Robinson assigns a lot of reading and if you miss a lecture, you'll be behind. She posts recordings tho so it's not awful to miss here and there. More work than my other classes, but genuinely interesting stuff. She's funny and was helpful when I had questions. Tests are HARD. Doable but difficult.

This isn't my first art history class (if you haven't taken one before and you think it's going to be a bunch of pretty pictures, you're kidding yourself) but it's my first with Dr. Robinson. While there's a lot to pack in, she is clearly enthusiastic about her topic and knows her stuff. Use the office hours and the study guides and you'll be fine!
The tests are crazy hard.

Genuinely one of the worst professors I've had the misfortune of having during my time at Vanderbilt. Lectures are absurdly dense but incredibly disorganized and lacking in any discernible structure. You never have any idea what content is actually going to be on the exams, its just a huge free-for-all. Do not recommend

Bogdanovic is a fantastic professor. I took her seminar Meaning and Form in Architecture, and it is one of my favorite classes at Vanderbilt! She cares deeply about the subject, the class, and especially her students. The class is discussion-based, and she encourages students to think deeply about the material and develop ideas independently.
Amazing professor! Tailors class to students' backgrounds, gives plenty of time for papers, no exams, super easy to access. 100000000% worth taking, HART major or not!

super smart prof- you will learn a lot if you put in the work. Lots of opportunities for extra credit. If you are willing to work, you can get an A even if the material does not come naturally to you. She is caring and passionate about teaching and wants to see you succeed.

I would recommend the BYZ Art course to anyone as Dr. Bogdanovic structures this class so that anyone can succeed with most grades coming from 2 essays you choose from. However, she absolutely knows what she is talking about and has high standards that could make higher level art classes with her difficult for non-majors.

Very sweet and exceedingly knowledgeable. There are two mandatory papers and a third optional, all of which are short and graded very leniently. She also offers 10-30 points of extra credit if you submit 1-3 short written assignments. Overall, great professor and easy, interesting class

Dr. Bogdanovic is solid, it's clear she has a deep passion for the subject she is teaching. If you ask her a question about Rome, she will go on a 10-minute insightful tangent. But, she and her TA were ruthless and harshly graded the 4 papers. Bogdanovic provided plenty of opportunities for extra credit, but they are time-consuming. An A is hard.

I loved Dr. Levin's class! He is super passionate about the content he teaches and his lectures are captivating. It is a lot of work--for exams be prepared to memorize a lot of information and the final project takes time. However, it is totally doable to get an A if you put in effort. You also don't need to do the textbook and he is accessible.

Worst class I have ever taken in my life. The fact that this course is offered at Vanderbilt is offensive – and he is 99% of the issue. His tests are easy, and the grading is impossible. He has expectations that are unreasonable such as grading down for not writing something that was not even asked.

You can actually learn a lot from Prof Levin's class. He will place some test questions on PPT, just make sure you read them. He will hold review sessions before tests, super helpful. Just get ready to read and focus in class, as lectures only cover a part of readings and will go beyond reading, but exams will test you on both lectures and readings

The lecture slides cannot be understood. He takes attendance and marks whoever speaks. Grades are never updated, so you basically never know your grades. He's accessable outside of class, but not helpful.
PowerPoints were extremely difficult to follow. There was never a rubric provided. He doesn't share his PowerPoint presentations online. When you ask the TAs they never know what's going on either.
im sorry but this is a test

Lots of reading for this class. His lectures were interesting but did not cover all of the reading. Tests were sometimes weirdly specific and assignment's were vague. However the content was enjoyable and as long as you do all the reading/take notes you'll be fine. Final exam was open note and he sends out possible essay questions for all tests

PSYPC 2200 - Psychology of Thinking

This class was awful. Professor Levin is a harsh grader with vague expectations, and the TA grades just as tough. Feedback is minimal, and exams feel designed to confuse rather than assess understanding. The unclear guidelines and extreme grading make this course unnecessarily stressful. Avoid if possible!

PSY-PC 2200; One of my hardest non-stem classes. Do-able but I needed 1-3 days to dedicate to studying for the exams (I'd read the textbook+take note of all the studies & go to his review sessions and got As on them) & you won't get most grades back until the end of the semester. He's also difficult to reach outside of class & feedback is minimal

Interesting stuff to learn, however is is a very tough grader and it is hard to know what he is looking for. There are no helpful clear guidelines. He does seem passionate about his research, but not about his teaching.

(for PSYPC2200) energetic lecturer, but horrible about giving resources and clear guidelines to students. Not willing to give clear feedback on exams, cared more about catering to himself getting through the curriculum than catering to the students (not very well organized, would often teach content until the class before the exam)

Dr. Levin is a great lecturer, especially on topics that he's interested in, but tests are difficult and grading criteria for projects/papers are unclear. I've taken several psych classes prior to this course, so I had previous exposure to many of the topics covered. If this is your 1st introduction to psych, lectures may move too quickly for you.

Levin is a solid lecturer and cares about his students, but this class is so much harder than it should be. He assigns a lot of textbook reading and expects you to know every detail of it. His grading criteria for tests and projects is very unclear, yet he has specific expectations that he doesn't tell you about. He is a cool guy and loves psych.

Levin has a nice charm in his lectures and gets really excited which makes the topics seem interesting. However, outside the lectures, tons of reading for every class, tests weighed rly heavily based on niche textbook concepts, pretty harsh grading, hours of studying for tests. Final was open note tho W. Nice guy, but getting an A is rly tough.

This is my rating for my second course with him this semester, I had very similar experiences with respect to being unreachable outside of class and having unclear grading criteria. No concrete rubric/description for any assignment, but it would be revealed later that he had very specific targets he expected you to know had to be included.
This course is not difficult. But it is not fun either.

The CS department is not known for being a social community. This class is a break in that pattern. Group presentations take up half of all classes and the discussions are interesting if you involve yourself. Prof. is kind and does a great job proctoring the class . The quizzes can be tough, but if you review the slides you will get an A.

She's not an amazing lecturer but she is clear. The content is just not all that interesting. She does participation quizzes but she gives an insane amount of extra credit opportunities to the point where you can do none of the quizzes and still end >100%. Allows AI usage on assignments. Everything is take-home.

Very good professor. Not sure why the rating is so low. She really cares about her students and often asks for feedback to make the class more engaging, including choosing interesting datasets for assignments. The course is mostly basic coding, so a little boring, but it's simple and useful. She's very passionate. I would take her again.

Not the best professor but the easiest class you will ever take. Use AI (allowed and you are supposed to) and turn in assignments on time and there is no way you will get lower than a 110%. If you see this as the baseline for your future in data science, don't take. If you need it for credit and need an A, must take.

She is not that great of a teacher to be honest but the class makes up for it by being super easy. She does have a bit of an accent but it is not difficult to understand her at all. She really does try to make the class engaging but it is just coding so there isn't much you can do.
A lot of the class is taught by group projects overviewing a section of textbook. Overall a great brain break and easy A.

I honestly thought she was so funny and this class is very easy to do well in - idk why she has a low rating. As long as you show up and do the homework you will get an A. Very chill class and I learned a lot about current events due to the discussion assignments.
She gives everyone A if you just participate and do the million discussion posts and assignments which are like small papers every other week. it is just busywork but easy A.
