This class traumatized me.

This class has been the most debilitating experience in my years here that I have had visible regressions in my physical and mental health. I know numerous people who have dropped their major because of this class and professor. Save yourself. Don't take this class at any costs
the tests are literally impossible no matter how much you study. worst class i've ever taken by far.

Worst class I've ever taken. Lectures are mind numbingly boring and he has a thick accent that makes everything hard to understand. Literally the only reason to show up is to do 1-2 completion assignments per class. The homework is hard and long, and not at all what he teaches in class. Also the tests are also impossible. Would not recommend

Nice guy, bad teaching. Only if you enjoy learning about mathematical derivation for everything rather than the actual science side of it, go ahead and take it. He's talking in a way nobody could understand. Plus, incomprehensible teaching, he teaches how to do 1+1 and expects you to solve quadratics on exams.
I have one word to say: no.
The first exam was unexpectedly hard, but beyond that, all exams were really okay if you study. Dr. Clements is also a good lecturer. Take her you won't regret.

I loved Prof. Clements for Orgo. I personally struggled in Ni's Orgo I, but Clements was the first chem class I felt prepared when walking into the exams. She is so caring and her practice exams are very similar to the real thing. Lots of office hours and some opportunities for extra credit. She does talk quiet in lecture, so sit in the front.

3 midterms (125 points each) and a final (150 points). Technically flipped, but I didn't watch any lectures and only went by the book. She covers all the important info in class anyway, with very helpful worksheets. The BEST part about her is that she gives 6+ practice exams for each midterm + extra review sheets, so you will always feel prepared.

Dr. Clements is a great orgo prof. She provides so many resources to succeed. She gives tons of practice exams which are rly similar to the real thing. Her class is flipped, but you don't need to watch the videos you can supplement with the text book. In-class she goes over the more important concepts and gives a lot of practice. Take her!

DO NOT TAKE KATIE CLEMENTS, SHE IS THE MOST EVIL ORGO PROFESSOR OF ALL TIME AND I HAVE HAD NIGHTMARES EVERY DAY FOR THE LAST 3 WEEKS OF HER FACE !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! NEVER EVER TAKE HER FOR CHEM 2222

Yes, orgo is hard, but she is such a sweet professor.She really helps explain things in simple ways, though I did find the exams to be difficult sometimes, you still knew what to expect. You definitely need to study extensively before the exam, rather than cramming information. Very accessible and understanding with office hours! do NOT miss class!

She's a great option for ochem. Her lectures go at a very digestible pace and she'll always answer your questions on the discussion board + in class. The exams were all fair and her practice exams prepare you well. The discussion quizzes were open-book, internet, and collaboration. The aktiv hw's are very reasonable lengths so workload isn't high.

Dr. Clements is a solid choice for orgo. It's a tough class regardless, but she makes the content very approachable. Lots of in-class practice + LA collaboration make lectures engaging and helpful. Exams are tough but fair and are very predictable. Focuses more on problem-solving than conceptual stuff, so great if you like a practical approach.
AVOID her class at all costs! my biggest regret in undergrad
Do everything you can to not take her for orgo!! She is one of the worst professors I have had in my entire life.

Dr. Clements is a knowledgeable instructor, but there is a pretty heavy out-of-class workload. Her tests are (relatively) predictable. The final is a mash-up of all her exams w/ predict the products, drawing mechanisms, and ranking things (for acidity/carbocation stability/etc.). Overall, the best advice I got was to do the Klein textbook.

Clements is the best professor you can take for Orgo 1. All of her exams are fair for the rigor of the content. Plus, she gives tons of extra help (videos on concepts, practice exams, practice worksheets). Just do not underestimate the exams. Make sure you do practice problems and study a bunch, as the average on exams was around 75%.

Dr. Clements's lectures are fill-in slides and can self-learn to a certain extend, but dont fall behind! Supposedly reading can be helpful. Organic Chemistry is much harder than chemistry. For tests 2/3, you memorize a ton of mechanisms for how reactions occur and then utilize those logical mechanisms find a pathway to turn one compound to another.

Orgo is hard but professor Clements does her best to set her students up for success. She posts in-depth videos online explaining all the topics we briefly cover in class. She is really helpful in office hours and never talks down to confused students like I've seen some other STEM professors do. She puts her cat on her slides, so cute!

Orgo is hard and there is a ton of information that she expects you to know inside and out. However, she is very kind and cares about the success of her students. She provides a ton of resources for test prep and to supplement the lectures. The LAs in her section are also very helpful. Her tests are fair, and she offers a lot of partial credit.

Fun class.  I got a B first semester and never got into the flow of the class.  Orgo 2 with Dr. T was a bit different - he was more clear on what the exams involved and was generous with extra credit and partial credit.  Some struggled, but seems like half the class got an A or A-. Read and do problems.

Great class. If you're looking for the easy A, this isn't it. But, I think I was challenged and learned how to solve problems in this class more than any other since I've been at Vandy. Dr. T has a high standard of excellence so you'll have to rise to the occasion.
Tough exams and very lecture heavy. Everyone I know in the class got an A or A-. So if you have a pulse, he's good.  If you want the easiest possible path to an A, not the right class for you.

First A in a chem class! I think Dr. T is great - his exams are very challenging, but he sets you up for success. He's fair.  Very approachable and willing to help. Only challenge is that he assigns too many problems and he expects you to remember everything from both semesters.

This was the first science class that I got an A in. Dr. T is great (and quirky). He really worked with me over the semester. Office hours are important - he will go over homework and problems with you. This was the first science class where I learned how to apply knowledge to solve problems. His test's are hard, but very fair.
Seems like a nice guy and a good researcher but his exams are a lot harder than the other sections. I would not recommend taking him if you are pre-med.

Dr. Townsend's lectures are digestible and easy to follow. He made an effort to get to know each and every student in his class. He wants everyone in it to succeed. He gives hundreds of practice problems and will sit and work through them with you. His exams are difficult, but reasonable for CHEM 2222.

I went to his office hours before a recent exam - there were at least 12 people in there and he handled it very well. He wants to help you, probably too much. Someone else said it, but he's a major researcher. So he expects everyone to take care of their business and will not stalk you about it.  Great teacher. Great to interact with.

Good class. Dr. T has a big brain and kind heart. He's full on OG / Uncle status at this point. I found the tasks challenging, but he does tell you exactly what's on it. Downsides - he moves at a blistering pace. He's also a researcher, so he is very busy. That said, I've been to office hours once a week and he's always helpful.

This has been such an enjoyable class. Dr T. is kind and patient.  I think he's rather funny and cool with a nerdy vibe. I've learned a lot. The material is very hard and dense, but I think he does a nice job making it digestible. He expects a lot. Lots of extra credit, doesn't grade homework. The mock exam should be in every class.

He's a major researcher, but is always at class and provides clear study guides for tests. He explains complex topics in accounting well - but better in office hours. Never avoids a question and tries to be helpful. He expects a lot but I think is fair. Not many assignments, which is good and bad.  Just 4 exams. Lots of extra credit, easy grader.

Dr. T is lecture heavy. Suggests and provides a large number of problems. This includes what he calls a mock exam, which is a duplicate of the real exam to show you the style and help with timing. If you do problems and read, you will get an A. But if you don't put in that effort, I can see how the class would appear very hard.

Heard that S-Class Steve was back in Orgo 2! Great organic chemist. He won't ever say it, but he's also famous. Super helpful office hours. Exams are tough, but fair. He legit goes over the topic of each question before the exam. He cares that you learn. Has lots of stories which kept me awake. Pay attn because you will see him in the gym and rand.

Orgo 3. He teaches it "Cal-Tech Style" where you get 1 month to complete the exams and can use any resources.  I like that he encourages working with grad students and professors to work problems. Very lecture heavy. Small class is cool. You will need to remember Orgo 1 and 2 as we learned 5-10 reactions per class.

Dr. T, aka "S-Class Steve" created this seminar to teach the chemistry of drugs and how it plays into gangs and incarceration.  This was his first time teaching the class. Although it was bumpy (speakers canceling) it was very informative and interesting. I would take more classes from Dr. T just based on how much he cares about us.
Was very unaccommodating. Was not flexible with illness or unforeseen circumstances. Blamed students for getting sick and missing class and then wouldn't meet to go over content missed.
Rude to students. Ruined my love for biology.
AVOID AT ALL COSTS
Professor Nordman is one of the best professors I have had at Vandy, and I'm a double major! I recommend taking his class
Professor Nordman is great
Professor Nordman is the best professor at Vandy

ALSO notice how all the positive reviews are from the SAME EXACT DAY like bffr there is obviously something going on. There is not way this professor would have a 3.3 rating and have a rating increase by 2.2 points without him teaching the class again.
Great course overall

Get ready to read and study. It is not a walk in the park, yet professor Nordman is a very incredible instructor. When I was having trouble, he would always suggest going to office hours. I ended up learning a lot from him and did well!

I recommend taking this class!
Professor Norman is seriously the best ever. I had a great time in this class

Please do not believe any negative comments about Dr. Nordman. He is incredibly caring and approachable.

This class is very difficult, but he wants people to do well. I was failing throughout the entire semester, and even then, he didn't give up on me. He encouraged me to not drop the class and helped me to do well.

Best professor for this class!

Dr. Nordman cares about each student. Despite other comments about him, I recommend taking his section with Dr. Brame. He is very knowledgable and although the lectures are somewhat vague, you can do well if you try.

Dr. Nordman exams were tough. The average for one of them was a 70, and he was proud of that. He does test based off exams and learning objectives. However, the types of questions he asks are in depth and require great application. One time, he asked an MCAT question, and the whole class basically got the question wrong.

Nordman gives good lectures and responds to student questions throughout. His portion of the class is less dense (still pretty dense though) but his exams are just as difficult. Don't have to participate in lectures but you should pay attention. Only real criticism is that his slides are slightly vague if you are trying to learn retroactively.

The Norman–Brame section completely ruined my interest in biology. The content is manageable, but Norman's exams are vague, misaligned with lectures, and unnecessarily confusing. He provides no practice exams because of his laziness & incompetence. He is often condescending when being asked and shows little concern for student success. Just avoid.

Dr. Jones is by far one of the best professors at Vanderbilt. She is always full of energy and actually cares about her students. The class is structured in an unorthdoxed way that makes it immersive and collaborative. Truly the biggest benefit of the class was access to Dr. Jones who has tons of office hours and provides countless opportunities.

Drug Discovery is the most unique course I've taken at Vanderbilt. While the 4-5 group presentations require strong commitment, the skills gained make it well worth it. What sets this class apart is Dr. Jones' dedication. Her guidance makes it more than just discovering drugs - it's about discovering how to think, adapt, and lead.

Drug Discovery was one of my favorite classes. It's a group project across the semester and if you pull your weight you will get an A. Definitely a different format than other classes and takes a second to get used to, but Dr. Jones is so caring and really wants you to succeed. There are 4 big presentations, no tests, can't miss more than 3 classes

Class is an easy A with time and effort. No tests and you pretty much dont need to learn random info. But you will need to meet outside of class constantly and put in a lot of work in the group project. This class is manageable if you have a good team. I really enjoy the way she has structured the class and talk about it in my med interviews

I did not have fun in this class. It was difficult information that I honestly was not interested in. Only take this class if you are interested in how a drug is made from having thousands of targets to passing clinical trials.

The class is basically a bunch of group work. Its pretty easy to coast if you do the easy parts and have a good group. Everyone gets an A but it is a ton of work and reading papers. Also the groups are expected to meet with Dr. Jones around 6 times outside of class but sometimes at the most inconvenient times such as Saturday morning.

There are four 25 minute group presentations, one four-paged single-spaced book report, and a final 2 hour group presentation. This class is hard. The material is grad school level but if you follow her revisions for the presentations, you will get an A. You can only miss up to three classes. You won't be allowed into class if you are late!

The whole class is pretty much just group projects. You have 2 books to read + a paper to write for each but she's just checking to make sure that you read them. Pretty much everyone gets an A, as long as you are not late to class (BE THERE BY 8:00AM SHARP) and contribute to the group project. Learned a lot in the course! Def take this class.

Dr. Jones is a baller. She does so much and cares a ridiculous amount about this class. Its an easy A if your group is solid and the class is so cool. She has 10 years in industry and really intimate knowledge about the topics. The presentations are pretty intense and she'll hook you up with all sorts of opportunities. A rising star for sure.

This class is essentially just one large group project with a few fairly tedious book reviews and blog posts along the way. Carrie cares a lot about her students and will give you ample guidance to make your presentations as good as possible. A decent amount of work, but if you have a good group, you'll be fine. Also, basically everyone gets an A.

Carrie, as a lecturer, is very good. She requires participation, and she helps make sure you understand the material (Easy A). However, she is not very accessible outside of class. She is very busy, and she offers you many things. But she can be tough to get a meeting with or get an email back from. Just stay on her, and she will be fine!

Carrie really cares for her students. This commons seminar is super easy, the only grade comes from weekly blogs, showing up to class, and a 5 page single-spaced book review. She lectures for about half the classes, and she takes us to different labs for the other half. Would definitely recommend. Easy A
Dr. Jones cares immensely for her students and it shows- great class and a great teacher.

Dr. Bollen is a great professor who truly cares about your learning. He gives you all of the resources to do well in the class (TAs who will tutor you, office hours, an AI tutor for the class, a printed book of all of the lecture slides & practice problems). Finance is a tough class but he makes it as accessible as possible. 100% recommend!

BUS 1300 was the single worst class I have taken at Vanderbilt. He is a graduate finance professor teaching undergraduates for the first time, and it was completely unfair. He taught like we were third-year master's students, moved insanely fast, gave practice exams that barely matched, and made exams feel unfair instead of educational.
Would not recommend.
Great Professor. Lectures are extremely boring if you don't enjoy the subject material. His exams are fair but homework can be frustrating at times.
He's a great lecturer, but a tough grader! Get started on projects ASAP and utilize both his and the TAs office hours.
Dr. Florian is an incredible professor, however, be prepared to work. Start projects early, go to office hours, and you will succeed.

Dr. Florian is an awesome professor and it helps that he's so passionate about what he does. He can 3D print literally anything and encourages his classes to do the same. The class is very project-heavy and I think some of the material could be tough if you've never seen a circuit, CAD, or code before, but overall it's manageable and fun.

Really nice and enthusiastic guy. Seems to care about students' interests and careers and how the course content can apply to that. He is clearly passionate about the material himself and has 3D printed himself a boat!

Easily the most interesting class I've taken at Vanderbilt. Florian is an engaging professor as well. It's a very hands-on class with several time consuming projects, but if you go to office hours and get ahead it's very manageable. The final project is pretty difficult but very rewarding.

Awesome class- can't recommend it enough, super beneficial and applicable. 
I had absolutely no previous experience in any area of the course, and Dr. Florian did a great job making the content accessible. Projects were time-consuming but fun. Dr. Florian is an engaging teacher with a genuine enthusiasm for the subject and students' learning.

He provides vague instructions and grades with excessive specificity. The grading often feels arbitrary due to inconsistent expectations. This makes the class frustrating and more challenging than it needs to be. If the class wasn't required I would have never took it, It's a major waste of time.

Extremely harsh grader for the most part. Wants you to write exactly how he writes or he's taking off points. Homework can take quite a long time for a 1 hour class. Ambiguous grading criteria for most assignments. You can tell he is trying his best, but it ends up being a very frustrating class when it really should be a lay up.

Best class I have taken at Vanderbilt. Dr. Florian is kind and funny, and you will learn more practical skills in this class than any other ones. Class is NOT easy though, especially the final project. Go to office hours if you want to succeed.

The course is poorly designed. The workload is sometimes simply unreasonable and he did not give any heads up beforehand. Overall, he frames the class to be for all majors but in reality he is extremely unaccommodating to non-engineering students.

Dr. Florian is incredibly friendly and has the most extensive office hours out of any professor I know. I will say though that you have to go to office hours for most of the big projects in order to succeed in this class. I definitely recommend this class and professor to anyone who's interested, but beware this class is not as easy as it may seem.

This class was incredibly interesting, and I learned a lot. However, Dr. Florian can be a harsh and unfair grader. There were times when people turned in similar work and received extremely different grades resulting from unclear grading criteria. As well, he definitely favors some students over others, so be ready to convince him to like you.

Must-take class for mechEs. Beware, not an easy class even with prior technical experience. Workload varies greatly by week and can be as high as 7 hours per week (final project is higher). Don't recommend to non-engineers with little to no prior experience unless you are ready to put in the time and work. Quality class where you learn many skills.

One of the best classes offered for both engineering and A&S majors. He is very passionate about the material and is always accessible for help on projects/quizzes. The projects can be quite tough If you don't have a strong mechanical background but help is always available. The lecture slides are great; the lectures are engaging.

This was a really fun class! We got to work on cool projects, like making a phone case and then a syringe pump at the end. It's amazing how many skills we covered, like 3D printing, soldering, and programming LEDs with microcontrollers. Overall there isn't much homework but he is a harsh grader.

Gonzales is actually so caring and kind to his students, and even though the material seems intimidating, he does a great job explaining it and always encourages discussions. There are a few very short quizzes and debates in this class, but he's an easy grader. He's really a great person overall and this class isn't difficult.

Professor Gonzales is funny, incredibly caring, and personable! I also found the class material to be quite useful. He prioritizes learning and is understanding of people's circumstances. I also felt that we had some great discussions in his class. The only thing is that he is so busy that it can be difficult to reach him outside class.

Professor Gonzales is such a gem. He is the kindest, most understanding prof I have ever had. He genuinely just wants you to leave the class feeling comfortable in Stata and has no interest in making your life difficult. This class was a breath of fresh air. Very manageable class!
The best professor I have had a Vandy this far. He genuinely cares about the well being of his students. Loved the class.

Dr.Gonzales is deeply invested in your growth. This course involves Stata but for people with no experience, he guided them smoothly to the final deliverable of a policy-relevant research paper. Great mentor and person to chat with.

One of the nicest Professors at Vanderbilt; however, this class was super challenging for me compared to any other MHS class that I have taken. I think that he shifted his syllabus this semester to make exams/quizzes more serious which definitely caught me surprise. Tough class but nice guy,
Dr. Gonzales is amazing! He is kind and caring. For example, he extended deadlines for assignments to relieve his student's stress.
One of the nicest people on this planet. Not even a PPS major and I enjoyed the class. Def take it

Dr. Gonzales is one of the best profs at Vandy. The class is easy, he prioritizes understanding class material over grades, and makes topics like insurance and policy interesting. He is incredibly kind, super flexible with deadlines, genuinely supports students through personal challenges, and makes mental health a priority. Take this class!

Prof. Gonzales is literally the best professor at Vanderbilt. He cares so much about his students, always willing to extend deadlines and accommodate students' needs. Additionally, he is incredibly knowledgeable and helps mentor students.

I cannot speak more highly of Dr. Gonzales nor this class. It was an absolutely wonderful class to just learn about health care and gain perspective on current events. The class is extremely chill; Dr. Gonzales really cares more that you learn something than the grade. Highly recommend this class to all who want to learn more about health care!

Healthcare Under Trump is a class that everybody who's interested in a career in health needs to take. It gives you an excellent, objective look a healthcare in the United States. Dr. Gonzales is the nicest and most caring professor I have had at Vanderbilt. He'll always listen to students and is available whenever needed.

Healthcare Under Trump is a must take for all MHS majors and anyone wanting to learn more about healthcare. Gonzales is probably the most caring professor I have had at Vandy. He'll give you extensions if you need them. You learn a ton, and if you put in work, you'll do well. He really just wants what is best for his students.

Dr. Joseph truly embodies what it means to care about his students. Readings can be dense, but his lectures help clarify concepts and are always followed by a good discussion. The course is not difficult, and I appreciate how he challenged our thinking throughout the semester, which enhanced our learning. 10/10 recommend taking a course with him!

*for AADS 2148. Prof Joseph truly cared for his students, is funny, and enjoys teaching. Lectures followed by discussion every class can be monotonous, but a great class for AXLE credit. Worth while to skim all readings (~70pg/wk), but there are no reading quizzes. 50% of your grade is based on a midterm and final combined, so exhaust effort there

Props to Vandy for hiring such an amazing professor. He is very understanding and truly cares about his students. He is also very funny and enthusiastic during every class, getting you to think about AADS in a way you both learn and find interesting. He is very lenient when it comes to grading and gives back amazing feedback. Highly recommend

GREAT PROFESSOR. Professor Joseph is very passionate and knowledgeable. His class is very engaging and mostly discussion-based. He is also very understanding and willing to accommodate his students. Highly recommend! My favorite class this semester. The topics and readings are interesting (you do not have to do all of them or read the whole thing).

Professor Joseph is not only an engaging lecturer, but is also one of the most caring people I have ever met. He truly wants his students to do well, not only in his class, but also within their mental health and beyond. Take him!!
Moreso concerned with teaching and getting you to think about AADS in a way you have not before than making students work for grades. Take any class with him!!

Professor Joseph is a kind and incredible professor who cares about his students. If you come to class engaged and ready to take notes, you will not have a difficult time. His discussion questions (which come after the lecture) also do a great job of challenging you to think in ways you've never thought of previously. 10/10 class and lecturer :)

Professor Joseph is by far one of the best professors I've met at Vanderbilt. He's enthusiastic during every class, and facilitates very engaging discussions. He's a new professor, but is doing an absolutely incredible job. An A is easy as long as you keep up with relatively simple readings and study your lecture notes. Wholeheartedly recommend.

I don't know where these angry freshman comments are coming from. I took Engelman's ethics course and loved it. The tests hard but I learned a ton. I still think about the abortion essay we read. Really fun and impactful class.

Prof Engelman is generally nice, but he just lectures on the chalkboard. During office hours, it was always a circular conversation of him not explaining anything. You have to understand the readings before you get to class. He is not flexible with extra credit or test corrections. Vanderbilt also just has no resources for logic. AVOID THIS CLASS.

I don't agree with the negative reviews... Prof Engelman is chill and accessible in class, and the lectures made the material easier to digest. He definitely challenges you with the exams and stuff but you get lots of feedback and room for mistakes. I'm not that mathy but I enjoyed the class a lot!

I'd really avoid taking Engelman. He's always on a crazy power trip. He's generally nice but unbelievably sarcastic and rude sometimes. Grading on the final was unbelievably harsh for a 20 point essay that he made seem was easy.

Engelman was often very passive-aggressive, political, and rude to his students. Unforgiving, and an extremely hard grader on the final, despite submitting solid work (many impressive students were given Ds). Fine if you put in work, but beware.

Professor Engelman is funny in class and very accessible during office hours. Assignments are just reading the textbook, which is lowkey optional since its a lecture-based class going over the textbook stuff. Grade is based on 110 and you only have to get 93 to get an A. Plenty of room for mistakes. Would recommend.

Super approachable. He replies to emails quickly and is always there, even if you are spamming him with your emails. He genuinely wishes to help his students improve at writing, and his feedbacks are very valuable. Robert's lectures are very engaging and fun, thanks to his energy and enthusiasm for philosophy and teaching.

Robert does not know how to teach. Absolutely saturated with unbearable lectures to the point where students were falling asleep every single class. Unclear expectations for exams, poor directions, harsh and sarcastic when answering questions, and entirely inaccessible in that he requires handwritten notes (no tech allowed). Avoid at all costs.

This is for EUS 2210. Professor Barsky is one of the best if not the best professor I have met in this school. He is so inspirational and passionate about the subject and infuses a feeling of passion for learning within me that I haven't felt in other classes where I have to care for grades. I learned lots in this class, and I hope to take another.

For FREN3891. Second class with this man, still adore him. Amazing, knowledgeable man who cares very deeply for all of his students and wants the best for them. Attendance isn't mandatory and an A is guaranteed, but for those who do care beyond the A, assignments are very interesting and worth putting effort into.

I LOVE THIS MAN. if you have the chance to take any class with king barsky, TAKE HIM. had him first semester freshman year and he genuinely changed the way i approach my college education. caring, enthusiastic, respectful, and an easy grader. he is probably the most interesting person i've ever met and knowing him has been an honor.

He is the best professor I have had so far. He is incredibly engaging and made my first semester in college so enjoyable. He genuinely cares about you and only wishes the best for you. If only he taught more classes up my alley because I would love to take another one of his classes.

Professor Barsky is an amazing professor who made an intro course that is (arguably) incredibly boring into the class I was most excited to attend. Discussions are great; they give plenty of unique approaches to the same topic (as well as how it relates to Chomsky) and breathe life into anything discussed, no matter how drab the topic.

Professor Barsky is truly a gem. He cares about every student and tries to get to know everyone individually. He makes the class extremely stress free and is flexible, allowing students to explore themselves and what they are passionate about. I highly recommend taking a class by him; you will not regret it.

Dr. Barsky is a wonderful professor with so many extraordinary stories and tons of knowledge to bestow upon his students. Any student would be lucky to take a class with him. He isn't fond of the usual grading-scheme and is willing to accommodate to his student's individual needs.
Best professor I have had at Vandy hands down. So passionate, extremely interesting, and very nice.

Barsky tries to make you think about topics in a different way than you've previously been taught. Every essay I wrote for this class was a first person narrative, even our research paper. He likes to add a fun spin where he can, and he supplements the class with stories from his life to build upon the concepts being taught. I highly recommend him.

He is a gem, in this class you are introduced to many popular classics: Odyssey, Inferno by Dante, Alice in Wonderland... You dont even have to read all of them (or even any) to get engaged during class. He also occassionally bring foods to class for everyone to enjoy. Final project was super fun personal quest and he gives everybody an A.

This is one of the best professors I have ever had. His lectures are so inspiring and he is so passionate about French literature/culture. There are three meaningful essays; no midterm and no final. He is very accessible and helpful.
I don't think you'll encounter a better or more interesting professor at Vanderbilt. He's an awesome and caring guy. I don't think I need to say anything else.
Super nice, gives automatic A on everything, good lectures, funny, definitely take his class!
Professor Barsky is one of the best!! You will not find a better professor at Vandy. I highly recommend taking ANY class with him as you will not regret it. The best!!!

Professor Barsky was an absolute gem! His class was super simple and only had a few grades but all were graded very easily (he believes in bringing out everyone's inner A by the end of the semester). Although the class was very simple, it gave me a better chance to sit back and learn and enjoy it rather than focusing on a grade the entire time!

Dr. Morales is an amazing lecturer who knows what he is talking about. He is very respectful of everyone's opinions and is able to showcase nuanced aspects of arguments. I have learned a lot from the discussions in class and I feel more confident in my ability to make arguments. As long as you put in effort, you will have a similar experience.

Morales is the best prof I've had at Vandy. Extremely chill, content is interesting, and he is a super light grader. All you have to do is show up to class. Its clear he just wants people to find rhetoric interesting. I wish I could take another class w him. Anything below an A in this class is purely a personal failure on your end.

One of the best professor I've ever had at Vandy!! He is not only an excellent lecturer, but also very caring and interesting. Very helpful both inside and outside the class and gives useful feedbacks! Def gonna take his other class too!!
He is really nice and the content is super helpful for any field you want to work in or honestly just for life. Not a lot of work, but the work is all "meaningful" I'd say. Definitely recommend.

Honestly, Professor Morales was one of the best professors I have had at Vandy. I had him for science, rhetoric, and controversy. He has great discussions in class and is very open to all perspectives. He was genuinely great and as long as you put in some effort (just go to class), you will do great.

Professor Morales is an excellent and entertaining lecturer who actually cares about what students have to say during class discussions. He is a super lenient grader and wants everyone to do well in his class. Definitely recommend him to anyone.

I truly could not say enough amazing things about Dr. Z. She is not only a great teacher who truly cares about your learning, but she's such a kind amazing person. There are 3 exams, no final, and one semester long infographic. I wish she taught other classes so I could take her agian.

Went to two of his lectures and self studied the rest. He goes extremely slow and does not say nor test anything outside the slides. Attendance is worth 0% of your grade. Youre better off spending lecture time doing something else and self studying lectures. There really is not much content and its quite simple (evolution, hardy-weinberg)

Overall, very good. Very knowledgeable and offers good help when asking questions. But content wise, his part of the course is much easier compared to Johnson but his tests can be tricky because the ease of the material makes you be less prepared for them. Read the lectures very well. But an A is very much possible with good effort.

Overall, good professor. Material is easier than johnson's section but exams caught my off guard because the perceived ease of the material made me prepare less for them. It is completely unnecessary to read the textbook for his half of the course. Do practice problems and memorize vocab (sometimes appears word per word from slides)

Better than Johnson. Moved slower and less content in this half of BSCI 1511. Just like Johnson's exams, the questions are oddly specific. Though, Rokas provided practice questions after each lecture, so that's helpful.

I really liked Rokas. I thought he was passionate and did a great job of teaching new concepts. People are salty because they thought his part would be a lot easier than Johnson's, and it is not. Rokas's slides are very organized, which makes studying for his exams easy. He also only teaches material from lectures (unlike Johnson) which is nice.

Whole class graded on 4 exams (3 midterms and a final). Questions are unreasonably specific and often test on rote memorization rather than understanding. Content was not overly difficult in theory, but this class made me dread biology. Countless hours of studying were not enough to prepare you for exams as you never knew what to expect.

Everyone exaggerates how much easier Rokas is. All of our midterm averages were low 70s. The content itself is easier to learn, but the tests are challenging. You should consistently put in effort, especially since most ppl want to drop a Johnson midterm. His office hours and worksheets are helpful. Textbook also has good practice problems, def buy

Good bio prof but nothing special. Preferred Johnson's lectures as they were more engaging bio Rokas wasn't bad. Ecology/evo is generally easier so you don't have to study as much. More mem/vocab and calculations-based but if you passed high school math you will be fine.

easier half of bio2, most ppl do better on his exams bc it's less material than johnson's half. honestly might not have to go to class, just review notes and do lots of practice problems before each exam. make sure you slack off tho bc his third exam low key caught me off guard and was my dropped exam
Why is his rating so high? He does not answer questions, and purposely confuses people. Do not take this class with him.
This class is unfair. People in Desantis's class have it so much easier. Do yourself a favor and avoid Rokas
Just take Hillyer/Desantis. Johnson and Rokas are worse in all ways
Very difficult. Does not prepare us for exams

Lecture's were not always engaging, but Dr. Rokas is very knowledgable in his field and the information he presented was very interesting. Don't sleep on Dr. Rokas' section of BSCI 1511. It still requires many hours of study and prep. Do practice problems early, and keep up/get ahead of lectures. Overall, I'm happy he was my Bio prof

Lectures are a bit monotone but easy to follow and well thought-out. Provides practice problems for math questions and posts his lecture videos + weekly review sessions. Tests are exactly what you expect, and it's clear he is knowledgeable about the subject.

Professor Blocher won't provide a grade breakdown on his syllabus, but know that only the project and the final count towards your overall grade. I turned in 20 assignments over the course of the semester and none were considered in my overall grade. I would have prioritized my time differently had I known, but he intentionally keeps it unclear!

Blocher's class is fine but his style is what makes it awful. He is an EXTREMELY tough grader and doesn't like contrary opinions. Also nothing in the class really matters outside of the final, did great on the group projects and 100s on quizzes and still got a Pass because the final was so strictly graded. Do not take this course with him.

This is a horribly structured class that teaches you how to use ChatGPT to code (you do not even learn how to code). Jesse acts approachable and yet he is extremely condescending and passive aggressive. It truly baffles me how a computer science professor somehow doesn't know how to work a F string and has to consult chatGPT. The worst class ever.
The professor grades the final exam harshly, making it count for 50% of the course grade. He rarely shared genuinely helpful business insights in class.

MGT 6534; awful class if you do not know how to code already, basically teaches you how to use ChatGPT to code. Relatively easy until the final individual project which basically determines your grade in the class regardless of your other grades.

Lots of reading is required!! Attendance and participation in class matters. But I would say Jesse is hard-working and willing to offer help. You can find him whenever you want through slack or email. And for case you wrote, he would carefully read through it and give feedback.

He runs a flipped classroom model with readings before class and exercises in class. I'm not the biggest fan of this model, especially for this class. The class material doesn't do a good job bridging between theory and real life valuation models. Good comprehensive and intuitive explanation of corporate finance theory, however.
Ghandi is super kind and, in my opinion, a great lecturer. Exams are not difficult if you study the practice tests. He is incredibly smart and a great professor overall for the economics department.
Don't understand the bad reviews. Class was extremely straightforward, just need to show up to class honestly. Tests are what is expected from the practice materials.
Lectures are extremely informative and detailed. That said, you need to S T U D Y if you want to do well on exams, because you aren't allowed notes

Ghandi is a great guy and very knowledgeable about Microeconomics. The class is difficult and his lectures do not always explain the material in a manner that is easy to digest. Thankfully, he is very helpful during his office hours and is a solid option for 3012.

Very helpful during office hours and posts notes online. Tests are quite easy with sufficient practice and have curves; they do make up large part of your grade though. He also makes connections from class material to real-world matters during lectures (ex. Tariffs, collusion, etc).

Ghandi's lectures tended to be a bit dry and on the boring side, but I felt as though I came out of each lecture understanding the material. He also really cares about students and is very open to questions and discussion regarding the material. Exams are manageable but they 2 midterms and final make up 90% of your final grade.

This class felt easy. The only problem with it is that it was a bit boring, since the content is pretty cut and dry. Ghandi is very nice and genuinely cares if you put in effort. Two midterms and a final and the grade pretty much depends on that. There is a decent curve, but Ghandi never posted any of the grades/ curves which was confusing.
Doesn't post notes or slides. On occasion that he does, the writing is extremely messy and unorganized. Additionally, he will take multiple days to respond to emails.

Ghandi was great, made the class super easy but only lecturing on the things he would test you on and made that clear. Also made material for exams clear so you knew what to study. Very accessible outside class and helpful. Very smart man too.

He's honestly a great lecturer and his exams are always very representative of the class content. One thing is attendance is mandatory because he doesn't use or post slides. His review sessions are so helpful and as long as you keep up with the content you should do well.
Gets the job done. Would not go out of my way to take/avoid. Its an easy class
Easy course, nice Professor, worst lecture
If only you attend the class and take good notes you will score well.
Rubbish
The worst professor I have never met! The professor never care about his students..
